Reporting directly to the CEO, this role is pivotal in shaping long‑term value creation, driving financial transformation, and enabling sustainable growth across multi‑market, multi‑currency, and omnichannel operations.You will bring strategic vision, commercial depth, and operational excellence to a business with ambitious global growth plans.
Responsibilites:Set and lead the global finance and procurement strategy, partnering with the CEO and Executive team to drive growth, profitability, and long‑term organisational and shareholder value.Act as the Finance lead for strategic initiatives, including M&A, market entry, and capital restructuring.Oversee financial operations across all markets, including reporting, budgeting, forecasting, pricing, and margin optimisation.Strengthen procurement excellence through strategic sourcing, vendor management, and contract governance.Drive digital transformation, embedding AI, automation, and advanced analytics to enhance decision‑making and operational efficiency.Identify, assess, and mitigate financial, operational, and strategic risks to protect assets and ensure long‑term sustainability.Guide Group treasury and cash flow planning, ensuring financial stability, optimised capital structure, and effective FX risk management.Lead governance, risk, and compliance, ensuring adherence to international accounting standards, tax regulations, and ESG reporting requirements.Manage capital structure and cash flow, including treasury, liquidity, and FX risk.Build and lead high‑performing global teams, fostering a culture of accountability, agility, and continuous improvement.Act as a trusted advisor to the CEO, Executive team, and Boards on financial, commercial, and strategic matters.
What You Bring15+ years of senior finance leadership in global, multi‑channel organisations.Experience in distribution, retail, e‑commerce, hospitality, and/or F&B is highly valued.Deep expertise in international finance, procurement, cross‑border operations, and digital transformation.Strong understanding of compliance, risk frameworks, and ESG reporting.Professional qualifications such as CPA, CA, CFA, or MBA preferred.A visionary mindset with the ability to balance strategic thinking and operational execution.

data.title: Head of Business Development
data.jd_text:
About Us:KPay Group (“KPay”) is a leading fintech company committed to empowering businesses of all sizes through simple, smart, seamless, and secure technology solutions. Serving over 72,000 merchants across Australia, Hong Kong, Japan, and Singapore, KPay helps unlock growth potential by providing a one-stop platform for financial management, business operations, and digital transformation. In 2024, KPay raised a record-breaking USD 55 million in its Series A funding round—the largest globally in the payments sector that year. The company has since been recognised as PayTech of the Year at the Asia FinTech Awards and named to Forbes Asia’s 100 to Watch list in 2025.
About the Role:As the first business leader on the ground in the UAE, you will architect and execute our go-to-market strategy, build and lead the early BD/sales team, and establish KPay as a trusted partner for UAE merchants. This is a true zero-to-one role: you will shape our market entry, define our sales playbook, cultivate key partnerships, and directly drive early revenue—laying the foundation for scalable growth. If you thrive in building from scratch, excel in high-ambiguous environments, and are motivated by creating something impactful, this is your opportunity.
Job Description:Build & own the UAE GTM strategy – develop and execute the market entry plan, including target segments, pricing approach, partnership roadmap, and sales model tailored to the UAE ecosystem.Lead from the front – personally drive early enterprise and key merchant deals while building, mentoring, and scaling a high-performing BD team.Design & implement sales operations – establish CRM processes, pipeline governance, performance metrics, and incentive frameworks to ensure transparency, velocity, and predictability.Forge strategic partnerships – identify, negotiate, and manage alliances with banks, fintechs, ISVs, and channel partners to accelerate market penetration.Own the revenue number – achieve and exceed quarterly targets for merchant acquisition, transaction volume, and revenue growth.
Job Requirements:12+ years in business development, sales, or GTM roles, with a proven track record of building and scaling revenue in early-stage or high-growth B2B SME environments (fintech, payments, SaaS preferred).Deep understanding of the UAE business landscape – existing network, cultural fluency, and knowledge of local regulations, competitive dynamics, and customer behavior.A builder’s mindset – experience designing sales processes, team structures, and partnership frameworks from scratch, not just managing established pipelines.Strong leadership presence – ability to inspire, coach, and grow a team while remaining hands-on in key deals and strategic conversations.Excellent communication and relationship skills – persuasive in both one-on-one and group settings, able to engage C-level stakeholders and partners with credibility.Resilient and entrepreneurial – thrives in ambiguity, learns quickly, and operates with urgency, integrity, and a merchant-first attitude.

data.title: Director of Business Solutions
data.jd_text:
At Workplace Options (WPO), we deliver human-centric solutions that empower people to thrive - personally and professionally. With a global team of more than 4,800 professionals, we provide real-time emotional support, therapy, coaching, mindfulness, and family services to millions worldwide. Since 1982, we’ve partnered with employers, health plans, and insurers to design flexible, forward-thinking programs that evolve alongside today’s changing workforce.

Today, we support 160 million people across 150,000 organizations, including over half of Fortune 500 companies. Now part of the TELUS Health family, we are expanding our global impact by combining decades of wellbeing expertise. Together, we’re building healthier, more resilient organizations—one person at a time.

Current Opportunity: Director of Business Solutions, Middle East

Location: Dubai

Required Languages: Fluent English and Arabic

Hybrid role - 3 days from office and 2 days remote

What you will do:

This is a high-impact, growth-focused role for a strategic sales leader who thrives in outbound business development. You will lead efforts to build and scale a strong reseller ecosystem, while also engaging directly in complex enterprise B2B sales cycles with strategic partners and corporate clients.

Your mission: identify high-potential reseller opportunities, open new markets, shape go-to-market strategies, and position WPO’s wellbeing solutions as a critical component of partner and enterprise offerings.

Responsibilities:

Reseller & Market Growth 

Identify, qualify, and acquire new reseller partners across existing and emerging segmentsBuild long-term, mutually beneficial partnerships that drive sustainable growthShape scalable reseller programs in collaboration with cross-functional teams


Strategic B2B Sales

Lead consultative, insight-driven sales conversations with enterprise and institutional buyersNavigate complex, multi-stakeholder decision-making and longer sales cyclesArticulate compelling, ROI-focused value propositions tailored to each partner


Collaboration & Enablement

Partner closely with Account Managers to ensure seamless handoff and long-term successWork with Product, Marketing, and Enablement to refine GTM strategiesBring market intelligence back to the business to influence strategy, programs, and KPIs


What Success Looks Like: 

A strong, qualified pipeline of high-potential reseller and enterprise opportunitiesTrusted advisor relationships with senior decision-makersEffective cross-functional collaboration and smooth post-sale transitionsConsistent contribution to program evolution and market expansion


Required Qualifications/Skills:

To excel in this role, you will need the following qualifications and skills:

5–7 years of sales experience (wholesale, channel, reseller, healthcare, insurance, or tech preferred)Proven success in B2B and enterprise sales with complex deal cyclesStrong prospecting, consultative selling, and executive-level communication skillsExperience in partner-led go-to-market environments (preferred)Fluent in English and ArabicWillingness to travel regionally


Why join us:

Make a direct impact on regional growth and strategic directionBuild and scale reseller programs from the ground upCollaborative, fast-paced environment with room to growCompetitive compensation with a transparent, performance-driven commission structure


What we offer:

At Workplace Options, we don’t just deliver well-being services to our clients, we champion wellbeing for our own employees as well. Examples of our benefits and commitment to employee wellbeing include:

Medical insurance, various leave plans, Training/tuition reimbursement, Gym Reimbursement, Wellness rewards, Access to EAP benefits and all WPO services, Mentorship programme

At Workplace Options, we are committed to and are accountable for building a workplace where individuals feel empowered to bring their whole selves to work, free from judgment or fear of discrimination. We understand that having a diverse organization is only the beginning and it will require nurturing and care to thrive. We will continue to take action to ensure we achieve equitable and measurable outcomes.

We strive to cultivate a space where diverse voices are not only heard but actively sought out and valued for the unique insights they bring. By embracing and promoting authenticity, we aim to build a vibrant and inclusive community that fosters collaboration, innovation, belonging and personal growth.

For further details about WPO please check out our website www.workplaceoptions.com and these short videos give a great overview of what we do Human-Powered Care and The WPO Global Experience

Workplace Options collects and processes personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws. If you are a European job applicant, refer to our Privacy Notice for further details (https://www.workplaceoptions.com/privacy-notice-for-recruitment/).

data.title: Business Development Director
data.jd_text:
Thunes is scaling fast rapidly across the Middle East and we’re looking for elite hunters to open new markets and close transformational deals. If you love building, thrive on delivering big deals, and want to sell a product that actually moves money globally in real-time, this is your opportunity!

What You’ll Do

Own the end-to-end sales cycle - prospect, pitch, negotiate, close, and activateBuild and convert a high-quality pipeline of fintechs, banks, PSPs, and digital platformsOpen new markets, landing strategic logos and unlocking revenue fastDrive senior-level conversations with founders, C-suite, and product teamsCollaborate with internal teams to execute seamless go-lives and expand accounts


What You’ll Bring

8+ years in B2B payment sales, proven hunter DNAStrong network across PSPs, fintechs, banks, wallets, e-commerce playersTrack record of exceeding targets and closing complex commercial dealsExecution-first mindset - proactive, resilient, self-driven, hands-onFluent English; other regional languages welcome


Why join Thunes

Uncapped commission with real earning powerHigh-growth fintech backed by global investorsSell a mission-critical payments network used by world-class brands driving financial inclusionHuge white-space markets - build and own your territoryCulture of ownership, ambition, and entrepreneurial execution


About Thunes

Thunes is the Smart Superhighway to move money around the world. Thunes’ proprietary Direct Global Network allows Members to make payments in real-time in over 130 countries and more than 80 currencies.

Thunes’ network connects directly to over 7 billion mobile wallets and bank accounts worldwide, as well as 15 billion cards via more than 320 different payment methods, such as GCash, M-Pesa, Airtel, MTN, Orange, JazzCash, Easypaisa, AliPay, WeChat Pay and many more.

Thunes’ Direct Global Network differentiates itself through its worldwide reach, in-house SmartX Treasury System and Fortress Compliance Platform, ensuring Members of the network receive unrivaled speed, control, visibility, protection and cost efficiencies when making real-time payments, globally.

Members of Thunes’ Direct Global Network include gig economy giants like Uber and Deliveroo, super-apps like Grab and WeChat, MTOs, fintechs, PSPs and banks.

Headquartered in Singapore, Thunes has offices in 14 locations, including Abidjan, Barcelona, Beijing, Dubai, Hong Kong, Johannesburg, London, Manila, Nairobi, Paris, Riyadh, San Francisco and Shanghai.

For more information, visit: https://www.thunes.com/

data.title: Head Of Compliance EMEA
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Who We Are:

At OKX, we believe that the future will be reshaped by Crypto, ultimately contributing to every individuals freedom. OKX began as a crypto exchange giving millions of people access to crypto trading and over time becoming among the largest platforms in the world. In recent years, we have developed one of the most connected Web3 wallets used by millions to access decentralized crypto applications (dApps). OKX is a trusted brand by hundreds of large institutions seeking access to crypto markets on a reliable platform that seamlessly connects with global banking and payments.

We are deeply committed to shaping a fairer, more transparent and accessible society through blockchain technology. This is why we publish proof of reserves monthly, and continue to ship new innovative security features.

About The Team:

The Compliance function at OKX is evolving. We are not just a control function; we are a strategic partner to the business. We are a team of risk-minded problem solvers and operational executors who ensure the business runs safely at speed. We advise on regulatory obligations while relentlessly optimizing our processes to allow OKX to scale efficiently.

What Youll Be Doing:

As the Head of Compliance, Europe, you will be the operational engine behind our European compliance strategy.You will move beyond high-level advisory to become a true executor, responsible for bringing the compliance team to its next level of maturity.

Your mandate is clear: make OKXs European compliance run faster and better. You will lead a diverse team of country heads and MLROs, transforming them into a cohesive, high-performance unit. You will drive operational excellence, reduce friction in our controls, and support strong compliance controls without slowing down commercial growth.

What Youll Be Doing:

Drive the strategic direction and the execution of the Compliance program across all OKX entities within Europe.Manage and elevate the performance of Country Heads of Compliance and MLROs, moving the team to a more unified, fast-moving regional force.Audit current workflows and ruthlessly eliminate inefficiencies. Implement robust, scalable frameworks that reduce manual workload and utilize data/tech to speed up decision-making.Liaise with first, second, and third-line teams to build a control environment that exceeds regulatory standards while maintaining commercial agility.Oversee compliance reviews and investigations.Produce data-driven compliance reports for management and the Board. You will focus on metrics, SLAs, and KPIs that demonstrate the speed and effectiveness of the compliance function.Participate in Board committee and sub-committee meetings, whether as a member or on an as-needed basis.Act as the primary escalation point for complex issues, providing decisive, commercially aware guidance to business stakeholders. 

What We Look For In You:

8+ years of experience in compliance within financial services, FinTech or cryptocurrency industries, with a specific track record of operationalizing compliance strategies.You dont just identify problems, you fix them. You have a proven ability to work fast, meet tight deadlines, and drive projects to completion in a high-pressure environment.Experience reporting into or working closely with Operations teams. You understand how to balance regulatory safety with operational speed.Deep understanding of EU regulations (MiCA, GDPR, AMLD5/6) and UK regimes. You know how to navigate the fragmented European regulatory landscape.Strong people management skills with experience leading dispersed teams. You know how to rally a team around a common goal and drive performance.You are comfortable using data to measure team performance, identify bottlenecks, and justify resource allocation.Crisp, high-impact communication skills. You can explain complex regulatory concepts to product teams and operational challenges to regulators. 

Nice To Haves:

Direct experience scaling a crypto or high-growth fintech company in Europe.Prior experience working in the crypto industry.CAMS, ICA, or equivalent certification. 

Perks & Benefits

Competitive total compensation packageL&D programs and Education subsidy for employees growth and developmentVarious team building programs and company eventsWellness and meal allowancesComprehensive healthcare schemes for employees and dependantsMore that we love to tell you along the process! 

Notice:

All official OKX vacancies are published on this website. While roles may appear on selected third-party platforms from time to time, information on other sites may be inaccurate or outdated. If in doubt, please apply directly through our official careers website.

Information collected and processed as part of the recruitment process of any job application you choose to submit is subject to OKXs Candidate Privacy Notice.

data.title: Director Analytics Business Engagement
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Join Us At Miral Experiences

Miral Experiences is a region’s leader in the management and operation of world-class, award-winning immersive attractions and experiences across Abu Dhabi. Our diverse portfolio includes some of the most iconic entertainment and leisure destinations, including Ferrari World Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Yas Waterworld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Warner Bros. World™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, SeaWorld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, CLYMB™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, and TeamLab. Phenomena Abu Dhabi, and CLYMB™ Abu Dhabi, as well as cultural treasures like Qasr Al Watan. We specialize in crafting unique experiences that cater to all tastes and ages, delivering a seamless blend of thrills, joy, and discovery. Our attractions are designed to ignite the imagination and create unforgettable memories for visitors from around the world. Whether you're seeking excitement, relaxation, or cultural enrichment, we offer something for everyone.

About The Role

As the Director of Analytics Business Engagement, you will drive the bridge between business strategy and data analytics to foster a culture of data-driven decision-making across Miral’s tenants. This pivotal role demands leadership in aligning analytics initiatives with business objectives, ensuring strategic insights deliver measurable value. You will champion stakeholder engagement by acting as a trusted advisor to business leaders, translating complex data into actionable recommendations. By prioritizing high-impact analytics projects and leading change management efforts, you will embed data literacy and governance across the organization. Your expertise will shape innovative solutions, optimize workflows, and position Miral as a leader in leveraging analytics for performance enhancement.

What You'll Do

Business Engagement

Develop and sustain high-level relationships with business leaders to deeply understand their strategic goals and data requirements, serving as a trusted advisor on leveraging analytics for competitive advantage.Align analytics initiatives with organizational priorities by collaborating with senior leadership to identify transformative opportunities that enhance business performance through data-driven insights.Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of cross-functional analytics projects, from ideation to execution, ensuring solutions are prioritized, delivered, and scaled to meet evolving business needs.Drive organizational change by fostering data literacy and a culture of analytics adoption, equipping teams with the skills and tools to harness data for decision-making.Design and execute strategic roadmaps for data transformation, embedding analytics into core business processes to accelerate innovation and operational excellence.Define and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to evaluate the impact of analytics initiatives, ensuring alignment with business outcomes and continuous improvement.Serve as the primary representative of the Data Analytics Department in executive forums, articulating insights in clear, compelling terms to influence strategic decisions and resource allocation.Optimize engagement processes and data workflows by implementing best practices in governance, quality assurance, and compliance to enhance efficiency and reliability.Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging technologies in data analytics, proactively introducing cutting-edge solutions to solve complex business challenges and drive engagement.

What We're Looking For

Essential

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Science, Analytics, or a related technical field.10+ years of experience in business engagement, analytics, or a related domain, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role.Proven technical proficiency in data analytics tools (e.g., SQL, Power BI, Tableau) and frameworks for data governance and warehouse platforms.Strong leadership experience managing cross-functional teams, with exceptional interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.Deep business acumen and strategic mindset, with a track record of driving performance through data-driven decision-making.Outstanding communication and presentation abilities, capable of simplifying complex analytics for executive and non-technical audiences.Experience leading change initiatives to embed data and analytics culture across diverse business units.Demonstrated problem-solving skills with a history of delivering solutions aligned to business objectives.

Desirable

Advanced education or certifications in data analytics, technical fields, or business strategy.10+ years of specialized experience in data analytics, with expertise in advanced analytics techniques and tools.

Shape The Future With Us

At Miral Experiences, we don’t just create world-class destinations - we create unforgettable memories for our guests and remarkable careers for our people. From theme parks and attractions to guest services and behind-the-scenes roles, every member of our team plays a part in delivering exceptional exper

Job Description

Aldar Education are currently seeking an outstanding Head of Chemistry for Yasmina British Academy in Abu Dhabi, commencing next academic year 2026-2027.

Yasmina British Academy is an outstanding English Curriculum school in the beautiful city of Abu Dhabi. With our globally recognised and admired curriculum, dedicated and passionate practitioners and outstanding teaching and learning, Yasmina British Academy is an exceptional place to work. It has an exciting, diverse and progressive learning community that annually produces some of the very best I/GCSE and A Level result in the UAE. Our learning culture is under pinned by our core values of excellence, respect, passion, collaboration and creativity allowing all students to thrive. Furthermore, the Academy is extremely proud of its recent ADEK inspection, which rated Yasmina British Academy as 'Outstanding' in all six performance standards, putting it amongst the top schools in the UAE and Middle East.

Responsibilities

 RESPONSIBILITIES 

Development of the department

Develop and implement departmental policies aligned with the school’s vision, high achievement standards, and effective teaching and learning.Establish, monitor, and evaluate short-, medium-, and long-term departmental development plans.Use data to identify underachieving students and implement targeted support strategies.Analyse school data, research, and inspection findings to inform teaching practices, expectations, and improvement targets.Ensure schemes of work are relevant, regularly reviewed, and inclusive of SEN, G&T, and EAL provision across all year groups.


Teaching and learning

Ensure curriculum coverage, continuity, and progression for all students, including high achievers and those with special educational needs.Establish clear policies for assessment, recording, and reporting of student achievement.Set and maintain high expectations for teaching quality, student achievement, and behaviour.Monitor and evaluate teaching and learning to identify strengths and areas for improvement, and implement actions accordingly.Ensure regular departmental contribution to the school’s CAS programme and after-school booster/revision sessions for I/GCSE and IB DP students.


Leading and managing staff

Performance manage team in accordance with school performance management policy;Establish clear expectations and constructive working relationships with team, ensuring collaboration, support, accountability and high professional standards;Hold regular department meetings with minutes and agendas; agenda should include focus on improving standards relating to student achievement and teaching and learning;


Managing resources

Efficient and effective deployment and control of staff, resources and budgets;Effective use accommodation to create an effective and stimulating environment for teaching and learning in accordance with Learning Environment Policy;Ensure a safe working and learning environment in which risks are properly assessed and Health and Safety policy and regulations are observed at all times.


Other Duties And Responsibilities

Contribute to the school's CAS Programme by leading/supporting one after-school activity each term (CAS includes I/GCSE and IB DP Revision/Booster Sessions);To be an Academic Mentor and Extended Essay Supervisor to Year 12/13 students;Cover for absent colleagues and invigilate examinations, as required, including a minimum of one evening and one weekend external examination session, to support the Examinations Officer and Summer Examination Schedule;Any other duties as assigned by the Principal.


Qualifications

 QUALIFICATIONS 

Bachelor's degree in Education or other relevant field with a teaching qualification.Minimum of 6 years' experience in teaching specialist subject up to GCSE and A Level desirable but not essential.Previous experience working with students whom English is not their first language.A high level of professionalism and consideration of the well-being of children.Respect for all members of a school community, irrespective of position, gender, age and ethnic background.A clean enhanced Disclosure and Barring Services check or police check.

data.title: Smart Metering Systems Project Director
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Talan is a global consulting group specializing in innovation and transformation through technology. For over 20 years, we have partnered with companies and public organizations to drive meaningful change.

We are purpose-driven, working across diverse sectors to tackle social and environmental challenges, simplify and improve market operations, leverage digital transformation, and ensure that data is managed responsibly and ethically.

Our expertise spans consultancy and outsourcing services, including programme management, market design, and governance. We also offer award-winning capabilities in cyber security, data privacy, and digital transformation.

We are currently seeking a Smart Metering Systems Project Director to join our team.

Job Description


The Smart Metering Systems Project Director will lead the delivery of the Smart Metering ecosystem, encompassing Meters, communications services, Head-End System (HES), and Meter Data Management System (MDMS). The role ensures seamless integration between operational and IT systems, high-quality field deployment, and reliable data flow from devices to enterprise platforms. It requires strong technical acumen, vendor coordination, and programme leadership within large-scale utilities or smart grid transformation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

• Oversee the full lifecycle delivery of Meters, communications services, HES, and/or MDMS systems, ensuring technical alignment and performance.

• Manage multi-vendor relationships and integration across system components.

• Coordinate planning, logistics, testing, and operational readiness activities.

• Ensure cybersecurity, scalability, and compliance with regulatory and performance standards.

• Supervise data integrity, validation, and transfer between HES, MDMS, and downstream systems.

• Report progress, risks, and dependencies to the Programme Manager and CTO.

• Drive continuous improvement and innovation across solution components.

Qualifications


• 12+ years of experience in utilities, energy, or large-scale smart metering or AMI projects.

• Proven leadership across multiple technology domains (Meters, communications services, HES, MDMS, or related systems).

• Strong understanding of communication protocols (DLMS/COSEM), data integration, and cybersecurity.

• Experience managing multi-vendor delivery and technical integration in complex environments.

• Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and delivery governance skills.

• Engineering, IT, or related technical degree; PMP, MPM, or MSP certification beneficial.

data.title: Facilities Asset Management Director (FM Operations)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Join Us At Miral Experiences

Miral Experiences is a region’s leader in the management and operation of world-class, award-winning immersive attractions and experiences across Abu Dhabi. Our diverse portfolio includes some of the most iconic entertainment and leisure destinations, including Ferrari World Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Yas Waterworld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Warner Bros. World™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, SeaWorld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, CLYMB™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, and TeamLab. Phenomena Abu Dhabi, and CLYMB™ Abu Dhabi, as well as cultural treasures like Qasr Al Watan. We specialize in crafting unique experiences that cater to all tastes and ages, delivering a seamless blend of thrills, joy, and discovery. Our attractions are designed to ignite the imagination and create unforgettable memories for visitors from around the world. Whether you're seeking excitement, relaxation, or cultural enrichment, we offer something for everyone.

About The Role

As the Facilities Asset Management Director, you will drive the strategic development and execution of facility management policies to ensure the seamless operation and maintenance of all physical assets. You will oversee comprehensive CAPEX and OPEX budgets, direct major upgrades and modifications, and guarantee compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations. Leading the Hard and Soft Services Divisions—including HVAC, plumbing, irrigation, and civil works—you will analyze equipment failures, optimize cost-effective practices, and manage a high-performing facilities team. Your role demands a blend of technical expertise, leadership, and operational acumen to maintain peak efficiency, minimize downtime, and uphold the highest standards of safety and performance across our parks.

What You'll Do

Facility Operations & Maintenance

Supervise and manage all facilities operations, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and operational excellence.Plan, direct, and control all aspects of leisure facility maintenance to maintain optimal functionality and guest satisfaction.

Financial & Budgetary Oversight

Manage Division Profit & Loss (P&L), collaborating with Finance and Senior Leadership to track expenditures against budgets.

Team Leadership & Development

Conduct performance reviews and disciplinary actions to foster a high-performing and motivated team.Inspire colleagues to achieve success and progress within the organization.Ensure training programs are delivered to meet annual and quarterly training hour requirements.

Contractor & Vendor Management

Supervise contractors and vendors working within the parks to ensure quality and compliance.Approve third-party contractor schedules for monthly manpower allocation.

Project & Asset Management

Lead project management for new construction and facility upgrades aligned with park strategies.Oversee facility and attraction installation processes to meet contractual, technical, and operational requirements.Participate in design reviews to communicate operational improvements to designers.Develop scope, budget, and schedule requirements for refurbishment programs.Ensure third-party inspections are completed and facilities/attractions are accepted per regulatory standards.

Preventive Maintenance & Compliance

Design and implement preventive maintenance (PM) programs and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for integration into the CMMS tool.Monitor PM tasks to optimize equipment efficiency and minimize downtime/breakdowns.Ensure accurate and timely data entry into the CMMS system.Maintain proper inventory levels for spare parts across all facilities equipment.Review Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and contracts in alignment with procurement processes.Ensure all Fire Life Safety Systems (FLS) are fully operational and compliant.Communicate scheduled interruptions to facilities equipment to relevant stakeholders.

Safety & Regulatory Compliance

Prioritize the safety of colleagues and guests by ensuring adherence to government health and environmental regulations (e.g., HACCP, firefighting systems).Address and rectify items listed in the Safety Log from HSE in a timely manner.Ensure all colleagues are properly trained and utilize safety equipment to reduce workplace injuries.

Strategic & Operational Oversight

Provide input for setting facilities budgets and actively seek cost-saving programs.Monitor departmental KPIs and strategic initiatives to drive continuous improvement.Conduct technical reviews of documentation and collaborate with maintenance teams for operational efficiency.Oversee asset management and inventory to maintain optimal performance and compliance.Implement comprehensive preventive maintenance programs and develop departmental policies and procedures.Ensure parks’ assets are maintained to the highest quality and performance standards.Oversee maintenance operations to achieve objectives for costs, guest service satisfaction, cleanliness, and sanitation.

Cross-Departmental Collaboratio

data.title: Head of Credit Decisioning
data.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Summary
The Head of Credit Decisioning has end-to-end accountability for the design, governance, and performance of automated credit decision frameworks across consumer and business lending products. The role leads the strategy, development, deployment, and continuous optimization of credit decision engines spanning origination, underwriting, limit management, pricing, and early warning systems, leveraging strong AI driven solutions.
This role is highly strategic and technical, combining advanced analytics, AI and machine learning, decision science, and strong regulatory governance to enable scalable, fast, fair, and profitable credit decisions. The incumbent will ensure that credit decisioning capabilities support business growth, digital transformation, regulatory compliance, and superior customer experience.
Major Responsibilities 
Credit Decisioning Strategy & Leadership
Define and own the enterprise-wide credit decisioning strategy aligned with business growth, digital channel expansion, and risk appetite.Lead the transformation from manual and rules heavy credit assessment to scalable, automated, and AI enabled decision frameworks.Establish a clear vision for real time, straight through, and omnichannel credit decisioning across products and customer segments.Build and lead a high-performing Credit Decisioning function, ensuring strong capability development in analytics, decision science, and risk governance.Act as the central authority on all credit decision logic, policies, and automation across the retail credit lifecycle.
Decision Engine Design & Architecture
Lead the design, development, and deployment of enterprise credit decision engines covering:Origination and underwritingCredit limit assignment and dynamic limit managementRisk-based pricing and offer optimizationEarly warning and pre-delinquency triggersArchitect hybrid decisioning frameworks integrating:Rule based strategiesTraditional scorecardsMachine learning and AI modelsAdaptive and self-learning componentsDrive in house decision engine build initiatives or oversee vendor platforms (e.g., OMDM, TRIAD, SMG, Probe, or equivalent).Ensure decision engines are scalable, resilient, API enabled, and capable of real-time decisioning.
Advanced Analytics, AI & Model Integration
Oversee integration of predictive models into decision workflows, including:Application scorecardsBehavioral and transaction-based modelsFraud and identity risk indicatorsPropensity and affordability modelsEnable the use of alternative and big data sources, including credit bureaus, open banking data, and internal behavioral data.Establish challenger and test and learn frameworks to continuously improve decision outcomes.Ensure explainability (XAI), fairness, and bias mitigation in all AI driven decisions.
Decision Governance & Model Risk Management
Establish robust governance frameworks for credit decisioning, including:Decision logic approval and change managementModel validation, monitoring, and lifecycle managementPolicy interpretation and implementation controlsEnsure compliance with regulatory and internal requirements related to:Model Risk ManagementAuditability and traceability of decisionsFair lending and customer treatment principlesMaintain full documentation of decision strategies, rules, models, overrides, and exceptions.Support internal audit, external audit, and regulatory reviews related to credit decisioning systems.
Business Enablement & Partnership
Partner with Product, Digital, Sales, Marketing, and Operations teams to:Enable fast and accurate credit decisions across branch and digital channelsSupport acquisition, cross-sell, and offer personalization strategiesImprove customer experience through reduced turnaround times and straight-through processingAdvise senior stakeholders on trade-offs between risk, growth, customer experience, and profitability.Support launch of new products and channels through rapid configuration of decision strategies.
Performance Management & Optimization
Define and monitor key decisioning KPIs, including:Approval rates and decline reasonsRisk-adjusted returnsModel performance and stability metricsAutomation and straight-through processing ratesContinuously optimize decision thresholds, cut-offs, and strategies to maximize risk-adjusted value.Drive decisioning improvements across the full credit lifecycle, from acquisition to early delinquency prevention.
Knowledge & Skill Requirements
The ideal candidate will demonstrate deep expertise in:
Credit decision science and automated underwriting.AI and machine learning applications in credit decisioning.Decision engine architecture and strategy orchestration.Rule-based and model-driven decision frameworks.Real-time and API-based decision platforms.Explainable AI (XAI), fairness, and bias management.Challenger frameworks, A/B testing, and test-and-learn methodologies.Integration of fraud, credit, and affordability decision layers.Regulatory expectations related to automated credi

data.title: Director, Destination & Placemaking

data.jd_text:
Job Purpose - 
The Director, Destinations & Placemaking is a new function within Aldar Estates, created to own the sustained activation, positioning, and performance of Aldar's most significant destinations. It fills a gap that currently exists between what Development delivers and what Estates manages: the ongoing identity, programming, and visitor experience of the destination itself.This is a senior leadership role with direct accountability to the Chief Districts & Communities Officer. The Director will build the function from the ground up, establish the JV partnerships that will deliver programming and placemaking capability, and use Saadiyat Grove and Yas Island as the immediate proving ground before extending the model across Aldar's broader destination portfolio.

activation, marketing, and programming decisions.Develop destination narrative frameworks and brand platforms in partnership with external specialists, acting as the primary internal client for all placemaking and branding engagements.Engage upstream with Aldar Development to influence master planning and design decisions before they are locked in, ensuring future destinations are built with programming and activation in mind from the outset.Lead destination strategy for Saadiyat Grove as the immediate pilot, translating existing positioning work and institutional partnerships into a infrastructure.Manage the placemaking JV and advisory partnerships, including the selection, appointment, and ongoing direction of specialist firms across Yas and Saadiyat.Develop and maintain a placemaking framework for each destination that reflects the distinct character, audience, and cultural context of Yas and Saadiyat respectively.Ensure retail and F&B tenant activation is aligned with tourism authorities, and urban planning bodies to ensure Aldar's placemaking approach is aligned with the emirate's broader destination ambitions.Programming & Activation:Lead the destination programming calendar across Yas Island and Saadiyat Island, ensuring year-round activation that sustains visitor interest, drives repeat visitation, and builds genuine destination identity.Establish, manage, and develop the JV and structured partnership relationships with events, entertainment, and cultural programming organizations, including ongoing partner selection, contract governance, and performance management.Develop distinct programming strategies for each island: high-frequency animation and entertainment for Yas, curated cultural and prestige programming for Saadiyat.Oversee the development and delivery of signature events, seasonal activations, and community programming that reinforce each destination's positioning and generate measurable commercial return.Coordinate establishment and ongoing governance of three core JV and structured partnership relationships. (Events & Entertainment JV, Cultural Programming Partnership, Placemaking & Advisory Partnership)Identify and develop future partnership opportunities that strengthen Aldar's destination capability, including institutional partnerships with cultural organisations, tourism bodies, and international destination operators.Represent Aldar Estates in JV governance forums, ensuring Aldar's strategic and commercial interests are protected and the partnerships deliver against agreed objectives.Over time, develop the commercial model through which Aldar's destination management capability is offered to third-party asset owners as a fee-based service line.

data.title: Design Director
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey.

The Project

This portfolio comprises projects either directly managed by the client team or delivered by leading third-party developers under the client’s leadership. It includes a diverse range of projects, spanning new construction, master planning schemes, and executive fit-out works.

The design director will be integrated into the client’s team, providing senior strategic design leadership across the client’s portfolio. The role will act as a trusted advisor to the client, shaping design quality, governance, and delivery outcomes while ensuring alignment with Abu Dhabi’s broader development vision, standards, and long-term objectives.

At the portfolio level, the design director will be responsible for setting the overall design direction, drafting comprehensive design briefs, and critically reviewing submissions from signature architects to ensure alignment with the client’s objectives. The role will regularly present design concepts and progress to senior management, lead multidisciplinary teams and external consultants, and maintain oversight of design performance across multiple projects. Additionally, the design director will ensure that all design solutions are not only of the highest quality and buildable but also sustainable and capable of delivering enduring value.

You'll Be Responsible For

Provide portfolio-wide strategic design leadership, setting clear design vision, principles, and quality benchmarks aligned with the client requirements.Act as the design authority across projects, ensuring consistency, excellence, and alignment with client standards and governance requirements.Influence early-stage decision-making to ensure design outcomes support programme, cost, operational, and long-term asset value objectives.Monitor design progress, performance, and key milestones, escalating challenges and recommending corrective actions where required.Support the client in managing interfaces between projects, programmes, developers, and stakeholders.Review, challenge, and critique design submissions at all stages, providing expert guidance on functionality, aesthetics, compliance, and buildability.Ensure effective integration of design with construction, procurement, and operational requirements.Lead, mentor, and develop high-performing teams, fostering collaboration, professional development, and design excellence.Act as a senior interface with the client’s leadership, government stakeholders, developers, and delivery partners.

You’ll Need To Have

Significant experience operating at a design director or equivalent senior leadership level on complex, high-value portfolios.A degree qualification in architecture with relevant professional accreditation or membership.Possesses an architectural background with experience collaborating alongside renowned architects.Proven track record of providing strategic design leadership within client-side, PMC, or signature architect environments.Deep understanding of design governance, design management systems, statutory approval processes, and multidisciplinary coordination.Strong capability in design review, risk management, and decision-making across all stages of the project lifecycle.Experience working with senior stakeholders, with confidence and credibility to influence outcomes.Demonstrated commitment to quality, safety, well-being, sustainability, and long-term asset value.Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to lead, mentor, and inspire teams within a secondment environment.

Our values

Safety first - Going home safe and well: We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team.

Client focus - Deliver on our promise: We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long-term relationships with our clients.

Integrity - Always do the right thing: We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations.

Create opportunity - For our people to excel: We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.

Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfill all the criteria, please apply, as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.

We are also open to discussing part-time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role.

data.title: Director - Managed Schools - ADNOC Schools
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Aldar Education is currently seeking a Director - Managed Schools (ADNOC) to support our growing family of managed schools in the UAE, commencing immediately.

The Director – Managed Schools (ADNOC) will be fully responsible for effectively leading all school improvement strategies, marketing, and enrolment activities of the managed schools and have commercial and education line management of all managed schools. The incumbent will continuously strive to exceed internal & external customer expectations, capitalizing the economic profit and return on capital investment, while embracing the Aldar Education Core Values.

Aldar Education is the leading education group in Abu Dhabi, we provide inclusive and innovative learning that inspires and empowers communities, Aldar Education aims to be your employer of choice. We educate over 34,000 students across our group of owned and managed schools in Abu Dhabi and Al Ain and as we grow so does our need for highly skilled, inspirational, and dedicated school leaders and employees across the group.

Working collaboratively, our purpose is to empower the next generation to shape our future, directly supporting the educational goals of Abu Dhabi’s government. Today, Aldar Education’s efforts are guided by the Abu Dhabi Economic Vision 2030 plan, which aims to build a sustainable knowledge-based economy through premium education.

We are a “Great Place To Work” and currently employ over 1,400 teachers to deliver the English National Curriculum, the highly regarded American Massachusetts State Curriculum, and the prestigious International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme all with an international dimension.

This is an exciting opportunity to join the highly successful Aldar family of schools and to help shape the future of Abu Dhabi’s leading school group.

Responsibilities

Main Duties 

 Provide robust and intellectual leadership, vision, and strategic direction to achieve the highest levels of performance and ensure a culture of continuous improvement across all key areas.  Lead and support managed schools in order to build capacity and capability towards meeting current and future challenges in line with company priorities.  Foster highly effective relationships with other members of the team responsible for central functions, (such as HR; Finance; Marketing; Enrolments and IT, etc) in meeting identified key objectives.  Work alongside the Director of Education and other members of the Executive Leadership Team in the delivery of the company’s stated outcomes against a clearly defined set of performance indicators and standards.  Work alongside the Director of Education and the Executive Leadership Team to deliver the education strategy, mission and vision for managed schools. Through direct reports, ensure schools are aligned and articulate the identified strategic direction and build a shared sense of vision and purpose for all schools and their communities.  To support and advise HQ departments and School Principals on efficient and effective school operation processes, strategy and development and any other duties as required by the Director of Education

Quality assure curriculum offers and champion new educational thinking and educational initiatives in conjunction with the Senior Executive team and schools.  Support Principals in the most appropriate educational strategies ensuring equity of educational outcomes to enrich the school as a learning environment for its students, families and the wider community.  To provide effective, innovative and professional leadership and direction for Principals and other staff coaching, mentoring and talent managment to achieve the best outcomes for children.  Provide support and guidance in relation to inspection planning, preparation and quality assuring the SEF. (School self evaluation form) 


Relationship Management

Represent the company at school functions, interacting with parents in a professional and collaborative manner to build trust in the company and strong community relations.  Devel

Why This Role Matters

If you’re driven by the challenge of shaping the talent strategy of a truly global organisation—and you thrive at the intersection of business performance, leadership development, and cultural transformation—this is a rare, high‑impact opportunity.

As ERM continues its rapid growth, the Global Head of Talent will be the architect of our future leadership pipeline, ensuring we have the capability, agility, and diversity required to deliver on our long‑term strategy.

This is a role for a visionary senior talent leader who can influence at scale, anticipate what’s coming next, and build a world‑class talent ecosystem across 40+ countries.

What Your Impact Is

In this critical global leadership role, you will:

Define and deliver ERM’s enterprise‑wide talent strategy, directly strengthening business performance and leadership bench strength.Build an integrated, data‑driven talent system that identifies, develops, and accelerates critical talent across every region.Shape a culture of career opportunity, ensuring ERM continues to fulfil its career promise for all ERMers.Partner at the highest levels of the organisation—including the Executive Committee, Global HR Leadership Team, and regional people leaders—to drive succession, capability development, and internal mobility.Lead and grow a high‑performing team of global talent professionals committed to unlocking potential and enabling organisational success.

Talent Capability DevelopmentDesign global talent solutions that accelerate development for high‑potential leaders.Build programme frameworks for successor readiness, defining the “critical next experience” for each individual.Own and continuously refine ERM’s flagship programmes, including Path to Partnership.Partner with Learning & Development to ensure skills development aligns with future business needs.Provide career and development coaching to senior executives.Lead and mentor a global team of talent professionals. data.title: Cybersecurity BU Head
data.jd_text:
Make an impact with NTT DATA

Join a company that is pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We are renowned for our technical excellence and leading innovations, and for making a difference to our clients and society. Our workplace embraces diversity and inclusion – it’s a place where you can grow, belong and thrive.

Your day at NTT DATA

As a Cybersecurity BU Head at NTT DATA, you will be responsible for driving the growth and strategic direction of NTT DATA’s cybersecurity business across the region. This role combines advanced subject matter expertise with direct sales accountability, overseeing pipeline development, client engagement, and team leadership.

The BU Head works closely with Client Managers, pre-sales architects, and technology partners to pursue qualified leads, develop tailored solutions, and close complex deals. The role also involves expanding market presence, building strategic vendor relationships, and mentoring a team of cybersecurity sales specialists.

You'll play a vital role in developing and maintaining strong relationships with new and existing clients, conducting workshops, and delivering impactful presentations. Your expertise in security technology will enable you to support the closure of sales, address conceptual challenges, and maintain a comprehensive knowledge of our product and service offerings.

You'll be responsible for creating clear account plans, identifying client risks, and using sales methodologies to drive the sales process. Your ability to adapt quickly to new missions and deadlines, along with your client-centric approach to finding the best-fit solutions, will significantly impact achieving our sales goals.

To thrive in this role, you need to have:

An advanced understanding of security principles, concepts, and technologies, including NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and best practices in securing DATA and IT infrastructure.An excellent understanding of the technical concepts of security solutions and the ability to provide technical consultation to customers.Proven success in solution-based selling and achieving/exceeding sales targetsStrong client relationship skills up to the C-level, with the ability to deliver engaging sales presentations.The ability to maintain up-to-date, accurate sales forecasts and close plans.Proficiency in team-selling approaches and collaboration with internal teams.Knowledge of competitors and the ability to apply successful sales strategies.Flexibility to adapt swiftly to new missions, urgent deadlines, and changing client needs.A bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or sales field is preferred.Industry/Vendor sales certifications required.

Workplace type: 

Hybrid Working

About NTT DATA

NTT DATA is a $30+ billion trusted global innovator of business and technology services. We serve 75% of the Fortune Global 100 and are committed to helping clients innovate, optimize and transform for long-term success. We invest over $3.6 billion each year in R&D to help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. As a Global Top Employer, we have diverse experts in more than 50 countries and a robust partner ecosystem of established and start-up companies. Our services include business and technology consulting, data and artificial intelligence, industry solutions, as well as the development, implementation and management of applications, infrastructure, and connectivity. We are also one of the leading providers of digital and AI infrastructure in the world. NTT DATA is part of NTT Group and headquartered in Tokyo.

Equal Opportunity Employer

NTT DATA is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer with a global culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to providing an environment free of unfair discrimination and harassment. We do not discriminate based on age, race, colour, gender, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, disability, pregnancy, marital status, veteran status, or any other protected category. Join our growing global team and accelerate your career with us. Apply today.

Third parties fraudulently posing as NTT DATA recruiters

NTT DATA recruiters will never ask job seekers or candidates for payment or banking information during the recruitment process, for any reason. Please remain vigilant of third parties who may attempt to impersonate NTT DATA recruiters—whether in writing or by phone—in order to deceptively obtain personal data or money from you. All email communications from an NTT DATA recruiter will come from an @nttdata.com email address. If you suspect any fraudulent activity, please contact us.

As a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, the role contributes to the development and execution of TTIO strategy, drives team performance and development, and cultivates strong internal and external stakeholder relationships to advance the University’s IPobjectives and reputation.
Major Responsibilities
Expert IP ManagementExecute the role of IP Principal, maintaining focus on both the operational and strategic dimensions of the function, and providing expert technical leadership in the management of KAUST’s intellectual property portfolio, commercialization activities, and highly complex IP matters.TTIO LeadershipServe as a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, actively contributing to meetings and the development and execution of TTIO strategy as directed by the TTIO Director.Oversee the health and performance of the TTIO pipeline, including prioritization, team workload and capability development, budget oversight, risk management, and process optimization.Provide regular reporting, insights, and analysis to inform strategic decision-making.Monitor and advance IP Strategy Team and TTIO KPIs, maintaining accountability for theeffective management and performance of KAUST’s IP portfolio.Contribute to the continuous improvement of TTIO strategy, operating model, efficiency, and effectiveness, and identify/implement innovative opportunities to enhance TTIO initiatives.Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including senior leadership, partners, service providers, and attorneys.Develop and maintain a strong external profile that enhances the reputation of TTIO and KAUST, including representation at external meetings, workshops, and conferences.Lead the development and periodic review of KAUST’s institutional IP strategy to ensurealignment with research, commercialization, and national innovation priorities.
Team Talent DevelopmentEngage with IP Strategy Team members to assess skills, capabilities, and development needs.Develop individual and team development plans to address capability gaps, cultivate talent, and support succession planning aligned with TTIO and KAUST business priorities.Conduct regular one-on-one meetings with team members to support professional development.

data.title: Director of Product Management, Data
data.jd_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Director of Product Management, Data in Saudi Arabia.
This role offers the opportunity to lead and shape the strategy, vision, and delivery of a data product organization in a high-growth enterprise software environment. You will oversee data platforms, analytics, and reporting products that serve both internal stakeholders and external customers, driving business insights and enabling AI-powered decision-making. The position requires a balance of strategic thinking and hands-on product leadership, managing a team of product managers while collaborating closely with engineering, analytics, and executive leadership. Success in this role will be measured by your ability to translate complex data ecosystems into scalable products, influence cross-functional teams, and define monetization pathways for emerging data capabilities. You will work in a fully remote, asynchronous environment that values autonomy, clarity, and high impact. This is a senior-level opportunity to lead data innovation that drives measurable business and customer outcomes.

Accountabilities:

Set the vision, strategy, and roadmap for data products, including analytics and reporting platforms that provide insights into operational and customer performance. Lead and develop a team of product managers, establishing clear priorities, coaching for growth, and creating an effective operating cadence in a remote environment. Partner closely with engineering leadership to align technical investments with product strategy, system architecture, and business impact. Deliver customer-facing analytics products, such as dashboards for efficiency, metrics tracking, value stream analysis, and security insights. Define and execute monetization strategies for data products, identifying target cohorts, packaging, and value propositions to generate new revenue streams. Lead 0-to-1 product initiatives, including advanced data capabilities that connect internal and external datasets to power analytics and AI solutions. Ensure internal data platforms support organizational reporting and decision-making by standardizing metrics, data quality, and stakeholder alignment. 




Requirements:

Extensive experience leading product management for data platforms and enterprise SaaS products. Proven track record of taking data products from concept (0) to scalable offerings (1), including vision setting, customer validation, and initial delivery. Strong systems thinking and ability to operate across complex data ecosystems, including data modeling, platform instrumentation, and AI/analytics integration. Experience developing monetization strategies for complex data products and driving new revenue streams. Director-level leadership experience, managing and mentoring product managers and fostering high-performing teams. Exceptional cross-functional collaboration skills, particularly with engineering, analytics, and executive leadership. Excellent executive-level communication skills, able to articulate strategy, impact, and decisions clearly. Comfortable working autonomously and asynchronously in a fully remote environment, with strong written communication and measurable goal-setting. 




Benefits:

Competitive salary with equity compensation opportunities. Flexible paid time off and parental leave policies to support work-life balance. Fully remote, asynchronous work environment allowing flexibility in location and schedule. Growth and development fund to support learning and professional development. Access to team member resource groups and a collaborative, feedback-driven culture. Home office support to ensure productive work conditions. Opportunity to shape innovative data products that influence customer outcomes and drive revenue growth.

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

data.title: Director of Mining – Major Programs
data.jd_text:
Job Description

WSP Middle East is seeking an experienced and dynamic Director of Mining based in Riyadh, KSA, to establish and lead our Major Programs Mining & Minerals business line in the region. This strategic leadership role will combine business development, project delivery oversight, commercial and financial management, and client relationship excellence to drive growth and successful delivery in one of our key sectors. As a leader within the Middle East region, with global connectivity, this role will require a blend of technical expertise, project leadership, business development acumen, and strategic client engagement.

You will be responsible for shaping the direction of the mining business line, building strong teams, and delivering complex, major mining programs that align with WSP’s global standards and regional ambitions

Responsibilities

Business Development & Strategy:

Serve as the founding leader for WSP’s Mining & Minerals major programs business in the Middle East, establishing market presence and credibility.Navigate regional regulatory frameworks, cultural dynamics, and client expectations to build a sustainable business footprint.Define and execute the growth strategy for the Mining & Minerals business line across the Middle East.Identify and pursue new market opportunities, capitalize on your existing relationships with government entities, mining operators, and strategic investors.Lead bid preparation and proposal development, including pricing, risk assessments, and contract terms.Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients, and effectively represent WSP in meetings, workshops, and site-based engagements.Prepare proposals, technical reports, and client-facing documentation aligned with project and business objectives.Lead the setup and execution of PMC frameworks for large-scale mining programs.Develop delivery models tailored to regional client needs, including owner’s team services and integrated project controls.


Strategic Oversight of Advisory & Design Streams:

Provide strategic leadership for the Mining Advisory and Design service lines.Ensure alignment with global technical teams and leverage international expertise to support and grow regional delivery.Drive integration across service lines to create a unified client offering – from pit to port, from exploration to closure.


Project Delivery & Operations:

Provide leadership and oversight for the successful end-to-end delivery of major mining projects including feasibility studies, mine planning, delivery, and closure phases.Implement and enforce project delivery governance, quality standards, safety protocols, and risk management frameworks.Collaborate closely with multi-disciplinary teams to ensure seamless project execution and client satisfaction.


Client Relationship & Stakeholder Management:

Develop and maintain trusted advisor relationships with key clients, government agencies, regulators, and industry partners.Represent WSP at industry forums, conferences, and stakeholder meetings to enhance brand positioning and business opportunities.Be proactive in identifying opportunities to improve client outcomes and exceed expectations.


Commercial & Financial Leadership:

Take full P&L accountability for the Mining & Minerals major programs business line, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting.Manage commercial agreements, contracts, claims, and scope changes to protect company interests and maximize profitability.Drive cost control initiatives and optimize resource allocation.


Leadership & Team Development:

Recruit, mentor, and lead a high-performing team of mining professionals across technical, commercial, and operational disciplines.Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, safety, and continuous improvement.


Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in mining engineering, Mineral Processing, Geology, or related field; master’s degree or MBA preferred.Minimum 15–20 years of experience in the mining sector, with exposure to consulting and operator-side roles in the Middle East region.Familiarity with regional mining regulations, standards, and market dynamics is highly desirable.Proven track record in business development and establishing new business lines or service offerings.Strong commercial acumen with experience managing P&L, contracts, and financial performance.Demonstrated success in leading large-scale mining projects or programs in the Middle East or similar regions.Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment and drive change effectively.


Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.

Join our close-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the worl

data.jd_text:
Saudi Arabia

Director of Communication

Organization

UN Development Programme

Posted 18 Mar 2026 Closing date 30 Mar 2026

1 - Position Information

Organization: UNDP Saudi Arabia, through Stars Orbit Consultants and Management Development (SOC).VA No. VA26/40Office/Unit/Project: KSA/Program/ 01004920Position Title: Director of CommunicationDuty Station: Riyadh, Saudi ArabiaOffice-based: On-site (UNDP and PSU)National or International Consultancy: International / NationalDuration: 12 MonthsNumber of Required Positions: 1Vacancy Date of Issue: 17 March 2026Vacancy Closure Date: 30 March 2025Expected Starting Date: As soon as possible

This vacancy is announced by UNDP Saudi Arabia through Stars Orbit Consultants and Management Development (SOC). SOC is a sub-contractor for UNDP Saudi Arabia, assisting the programme in implementing their activities through HR and contract management of consultants working in their support.

To review the full job description, please click on the following link:

https://drive.google.com/file/d/19OsO9J1MkqNQMtfDBPw2cTR4w8IUpJWc/view?usp=sharing

2 - Project Title

Establishment of and Support to Public Diplomacy Think Tank, Prince Sultan University (PSU), (Project Number: 01004920).

3 - Project Description

Through the partnership between the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and Prince Sultan University (PSU), this project supports the establishment and operationalisation of the Public Diplomacy Think Tank in Saudi Arabia. The initiative represents Phase II of a multi-phase cooperation, building on the successful completion of Phase I, which defined the strategic foundation and institutional design of the Think Tank.

The Think Tank will strengthen Saudi Arabia’s capacity for public diplomacy by advancing research, innovation, and capacity development aligned with Vision 2030. It will serve as a centre of excellence to promote thought leadership, knowledge exchange, and cross-sector partnerships, positioning PSU as a national and regional leader in public diplomacy.

UNDP provides technical and operational support to ensure the Think Tank’s establishment adheres to international standards, integrates sustainable governance systems, and strengthens PSU’s institutional capacity. PSU will lead in developing research, training, and outreach programmes to enhance Saudi Arabia’s influence, promote global dialogue, and develop local expertise in digital diplomacy and soft power strategies.

The project contributes to the UNSDCF Outcome 4: fostering peaceful, just, and inclusive societies and mobilizing means required for the implementation of Agenda 2030. It aligns with UNDP Strategic Plan Output 3.1 (economic diversification through research, innovation, and digital transformation) and CPD Output 4.3 (strengthening institutional and technical capacities of civil society and academic partners).

4- Scope of Work

Planning, Coordination, and GovernancePrepare periodic communication plans (annual / quarterly).Monitor overall effectiveness and coherence of communication efforts (high-level, non-technical).Act as the central coordination point for communication across management, research teams, and relevant partners.Ensure communication outputs adhere to institutional and governance standards.

data.title: Director, Navy Portfolio (TS/SCI)
data.jd_text:
Position Title:  Director, Navy Portfolio

 Departmental Function(s):  Growth Leader (BD Executive), Senior Program Manager

Location: Remote - US; travel to Pax River, MD / Indian Head, MD and the Pentagon as Required

Clearance: DoD/DoW TS/SCI Required; will consider Top Secret on case by case basis

Do you thrive on creating unique and impactful value for Federal clients? Are you passionate about partnering with clients and collaborating to create solutions? Do you enjoy the opportunity to drive change that impacts our nation? We are looking for smart, curious, driven individuals to join us in partnering with our client to help them solve some of the nation’s most challenging problems. ASI Government serves as a trusted adviser to government leaders seeking improved performance, enhanced organization effectiveness, and strategic change.

Company Overview

ASI Government provides government leaders with the expertise they need to navigate acquisition and organizational challenges so they can make an impact and achieve their goals. For over 30 years, ASI has transformed the way government does business, impacting over 70 government agencies across the civilian, defense, and intelligence communities.

Job Description

We are seeking a battle‑tested Navy executive leader to own, grow, and scale our Navy portfolio. This role is designed for a senior professional with deep Navy experience, trusted relationships across the Navy ecosystem, and a proven ability to turn access into growth.

This leader will sit at the intersection of strategy, mission delivery, and business expansion / development serving as:

Director of Navy portfolio growth Business Development / Growth Leadership Program Manager for a flagship Navy Portfolio Acquisition Executives (PAE) initiative

This is a high‑impact, high‑visibility leadership role with direct influence on company growth, client trust, and mission outcomes. This is not a caretaker position. This is a build-and-lead role for someone who wants to shape strategy, drive growth, and leave a lasting impact on Navy mission success. You will have executive visibility, real authority, and the opportunity to scale a portfolio with momentum.

Key Responsibilities

Navy Portfolio Leadership & Growth

Own the overall Navy portfolio strategy, growth plan, and execution.  Ability to be the BD / Account Executive for the Navy Portfolio with your vast Navy Network; leaning on your previous connections and leadership experience from past engagements. Leverage established Navy relationships (uniformed, civilian, and industry) to expand footprint, influence, and revenue. Serve as a trusted executive partner to Navy clients, anticipating needs and positioning the company ahead of demand. Identify, shape, and support new business opportunities, including capture strategy, client engagement, and solution framing. Represent the company with credibility and authority across Navy commands and partner organizations. 

Program Management & Delivery

 Serve as Program Manager for a high-visibility Navy PAE initiative  Ensure excellence across performance, schedule, budget, and delivery.  Lead senior program teams and ensure outcomes align with Navy mission priorities.  Mentor, guide and advise Engagement Leads, Project Managers and supporting staff to delivery world class delivery with the support of ASI’s Executive Team. 

Minimum Requirements

 Current and Active DOD/DOW TS/SCI, willing to consider Top Secret clearances on case by case basis. “SCI eligibility” required. Prior Navy experience (uniformed or civilian) with deep understanding of Navy culture, decision-making, and mission execution. Strong, current Navy relationships with the credibility to operate at senior officers and executives. Ability to gain trust immediately with your knowledge and reputation gained throughout your civilian and/or military career as trusted leader. Prior experience as a Business Development or Account Executive within the Navy, NAVIR community. Foundational – Expert knowledge of the Navy Acquisition and Procurement process both traditional (FAR based) and non-traditional (OT / OTA’s) mechanisms. High business acumen and understanding of the complexities the comes with the Warfighter (i.e. Navy Munitions or related) procurement and R&D process. Natural passion and drive to deliver real value to the Navy Warfighter programs and brings a new, inspiring energy to help facilitate and modernize the federal acquisition process via the PAE. Proven experience leading large, complex Navy programs as a Program Manager or senior executive,Interest in close engagement and collaboration with our AI and Data Practice teams.  Track record of driving growth within Navy or DoD environments; High Win-RateActive Secret clearanceWillingness to travel to Pax River, MD

Preferred Qualifications

 Former senior Navy officer (O-5/6 levels) or Senior Navy civilian (Growth Focused).  Located in or around Pentagon, Indian Head, MD, or Pax R
