data.title: Commercial Director
data.jd_text:
The Commercial Director, O&M EMEA is responsible for developing a commercial strategy covering all segments and channels to maximize total revenue and profit performance as well as market share penetration of the hotel.

What will I be doing?

The Commercial Director, O&M EMEA is responsible for developing a commercial strategy covering all segments and channels to maximize total revenue and profit performance as well as market share penetration of the hotel in EMEA.

This role will drive the planning and execution of the commercial strategy and activity through supporting the Revenue management team, Sales, Catering and Events, Marketing, eCommerce and Public Relations teams, through working effectively with area support teams. F&B commercial activities will be shared responsibilities with the F&B Manager, Director of Operations, GM and the Commercial Director.

The Commercial Director is a role model for the commercial community within the owned and managed portfolio in EMEA, and develops teams and individuals in these functions whilst being a Key business partner to the Hotel General Manager. They support hotels in deriving the most value from Hilton Sales teams. The Commercial Directors role is key to seize new opportunities through strong customer relations and works as an Ambassador for the company with our Owners and Key Stakeholders.

The role ensures that commercial management strategies are set for all revenue streams and that all systems are used to their full potential to yield the highest possible amount of revenue from all available space, whether in Rooms, Meeting Space, outlets or other revenue departments. Driving overall FMS results gaining an Unfair share through a disciplined execution of commercial activities.

A Commercial Director will be lead, coach, develop, recruit and retain future talents. He/she will manage performance, develop and evaluate the commercial team members on the agreed KPI’s, whilst working effectively as part of a 4D structure on property. Specifically, a Commercial Director will perform the following tasks to the highest standards:

Directly accountable for driving the Top line Budget and Forecast by implementing a commercial strategy and business plan through to execution using the Hilton Commercial Focus process and all available business tools and intelligence available. Deliver as a minimum the budgeted revenues across all revenue streams, To include but not limited; All Rooms, GC&E, F&B, Leisure and Spa with a control on cost of sale, route to market, channel shift and marketing / Ecommerce opportunities.Work with the GM, Finance and Operations Director to ensure profits are maximized in line with TGOP and EBITDA targets set.Ensure Business Review Guidelines (current to + 5 years) all Pricing & Rate Quotation Strategies are in place in order to maximize all pricing components of sales and catering opportunities and achieve positive group market share.Support various On Property, RDOS’s, and Area Marketing teams in coordinating hotel level marketing, sales and public relations activities. Liaise with regional support and brand teams to provide maximum benefit to hotel performance.Support the Cluster Revenue Managers/On Property Revenue Manager and RMCC to establish an optimal mix, review and validate forecasts, develop strategies for different demand periods, and review and approve retail and group pricing strategies.Develop and maintain strong relationships with all stakeholders, owners, hotel teams, Hilton matrix support teams to ensure commercial benefits of the hotel.Liaise with VP of Operations, presents commercial strategies to Regional Team and key stakeholders.Consistently conforms to Hilton brand standards and corporate identity and utilizes all communication tools (under the guidance of regional marketing and BPS).Keeps in touch with Market trends and review/amend strategies accordingly in line with the evolving market conditions.Participate in the leadership activity of the Hotel and Region.


What are we looking for?

A Commercial Director serving Hilton brands is always working on behalf of our Guests and working with other Team Members. To successfully fill this role, you should maintain the attitude, behaviours, skills, and values that follow:

Minimum of 3+ year’s hospitality/travel industry experience in a revenue generating or commercial services role.Minimum of 3+ years of experience presenting sales plans, presentations, etc. to senior level executives and constituent groups3+ experience working in a collaborative/matrixed environment3+ years working with departmental financial data to make strategic/tactical decisions3+ years of experience evaluating and identifying business opportunities for a businessAt least 3 years of experience managing a sales or commercial teamFluent in English


It would be advantageous in this position for you to demonstrate the following capabilities and distinctions:

3+ years working in a multi-complex hotel/tr

data.title: Director Transaction Advisory
data.jd_text:
Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional

From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it will be anything but ordinary.

Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional. 

From the projects you will work on to the clients we work with. From career-defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where we all contribute to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it’ll be anything but ordinary.

Your next role

Buro Happold is supporting the transformation of cities and public services across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the wider GCC, aligned with Vision 2030, national transformation programmes, and public‑sector reform agendas. As Director – Transaction Advisory within our Municipal Services team, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping, structuring, and delivering complex public‑sector transactions across the region.

Based in KSA, you will lead Transaction Advisory services across priority municipal sectors including urban services, mobility, utilities, public realm, mixed-use development, sport and social infrastructure. You will work closely with senior public‑sector stakeholders and delivery entities.

This role offers a rare opportunity to combine deep KSA market understanding, strong knowledge of the local NCP PPP lifecycle framework, and global best practice to help public authorities structure bankable, resilient, and outcome‑driven transactions that deliver long‑term value for citizens.

Your key duties

As a Director, you will provide senior leadership to our Transaction Advisory and PPP offering within Municipal Services, driving growth, credibility, and excellence in delivery across KSA and the GCC.

You will:

Lead the Transaction Advisory strategy in KSA and the GCC for public‑sector projects, aligned with Buro Happold’s regional growth.Originate and lead PPP, privatization, and transaction advisory engagements, including project screening, feasibility studies, business cases, value‑for‑money assessments, and procurement strategies.Advise public‑sector clients across the full PPP lifecycle, including project identification, structuring, market sounding, procurement, bid evaluation, and support through financial close.Act as a trusted advisor to senior government, municipal, and delivery authority leadership, supporting decisions on service delivery models, private‑sector participation, and long‑term operating arrangements.Provide expert advice on commercial, contractual, and risk‑allocation structures, ensuring transactions are bankable, compliant with KSA frameworks, and aligned with policy and affordability objectives.Work closely with multidisciplinary teams (engineering, planning, operations, digital, economics) to deliver integrated, technically robust, and commercially sound transaction solutions.Lead high‑level client engagements, workshops, negotiations, and market‑facing interactions with government entities, investors, developers, operators, and lenders.Drive business development across KSA and the GCC, including strategic client relationships, opportunity origination, bid leadership, and framework participation.Represent Buro Happold with NCP, municipalities, ministries, and key industry stakeholders, strengthening our position as a trusted advisor in PPP and public‑sector transactions.Mentor and develop regional advisory talent, building a strong, locally grounded Transaction Advisory capability within the Municipal Services team.Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring quality assurance, commercial success, and effective risk management across advisory commissions.


Your Skills And Experience

You will bring a combination of regional credibility, transaction expertise, and senior‑level leadership experience.

You will have:

A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related discipline; a postgraduate qualification is highly desirable.Significant experience in Transaction Advisory, PPP, or Commercial Advisory, gained within a leading consultancy, advisory firm, or financial institution.Strong, demonstrable experience in the KSA market, including a clear understanding of government structures, procurement regulations, and the NCP PPP process/Framework.A proven track record delivering PPP, privatization, or investment transactions across municipal, infrastructure, or public‑service sectors in KSA and/or the GCC.Deep understanding of project delivery models, operating models, funding and financing structures, and risk allocation in public‑sector transactions.Established relationships with government entities, NCP, municipalities, investors, developers, and operators in the region.Strong leadership and people‑development skills,

data.jd_text:
About usEPAM Systems is a leading global provider of digital platform engineering and software development services. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Newtown, Pennsylvania, EPAM has been helping clients navigate digital transformation for over 30 years, delivering innovative solutions to stay competitive in constantly evolving markets.As consultants, designers, architects, engineers and trainers, at EPAM, we specialize in building long-term partnerships, enabling businesses to reimagine themselves through a digital lens. In Saudi Arabia, EPAM operates in Riyadh and Al Khobar, offering cutting-edge engineering, strategy, design, consulting, and innovation services across industries.  EPAM is driving digital transformation across the Middle East and we’re expanding our Data Practice in Saudi Arabia to meet the rising demand for cutting-edge Generative AI (Gen AI) and advanced data solutions.
We are looking for an experienced Principal (Director) Data Science Consultant Practice Lead with a strong focus on Gen AI applications across industries. In this role, you will design, deploy and optimize AI-driven solutions, helping organizations unlock new opportunities and navigate the evolving AI landscape.
If you are passionate about leveraging AI to solve complex business challenges and create real impact, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of AI-driven innovation
The role offers exciting opportunities to play a highly visible role in partnership with the wider European data leadership teams to drive growth in the region.

Experience in IT consulting is highly desirable Able to act as a diving force in developing and expanding the Middle East Practice footprint in partnership with the wider Data leadership teams Expertise in Gen AI, including Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and advanced AI applications Strong understanding of AI/ML tools and platforms such as Azure, AWS, GCP, Databricks, MLFlow and Streamlit Proficiency in Python and experience with frameworks like Airflow, Plotly Dash or similar tools Proven track record of implementing responsible AI practices, addressing bias and ensuring model explainability Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex AI concepts into actionable insights for senior business leaders Familiarity with AI regulatory frameworks Experience in pre-sales or supporting sales teams We offerGeneral Organization for Social Insurance (GOSI)Private healthcare and life insuranceEnd of service gratuityAnnual air travel allowance for expatriatesCorporate Programs including Employee Referral Program with rewardsLearning and development opportunities including in-house training and coaching, professional certifications, over 22,000 courses on LinkedIn Learning Solutions and much more Nature of Work and Working Hours The usual working hours for full-time roles at EPAM in Saudi Arabia are from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, including a one-hour unpaid lunch break. data.title: Creative Director (Digital Experience Design)
data.jd_text:
The Creative Director of Digital Experience Design is a key leadership role within our growing inter-disciplinary Riyadh studio to create the next generation of Destination, Brand Activation and Media & Content experiences across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, where you will be working closely with other creative directors and the wider group on the visioning and conception of highly experiential immersive environments, agnostic to scale and channel. 
QSAS prides itself in building a creative department of ‘T–Shaped’ people, meaning that while you will have a core specialism in digital interactive engagement and experiential design at which you will excel, your role will also include being asked to think across various design disciplines to assist and guide in the envisioning, development, production and delivery of physical and virtually-integrated attractions.
As a Creative Director you will report in directly to the head of creative and lead a team of senior, mid and junior creative personnel within the studio. You will possess exceptionally strong leadership skills, someone who thrives in collaborative environments as a team player with an innate ability to unlock, develop and nurture the team’s competencies to elevate our creative product offering. 
Your core strengths should be rooted in the generation of Big Ideas, highly-conceptual design thinking, narrative ideation and overarching story-telling experience visioning. This will include all forms of digital communication touch-points, interactive media design, art direction, motion content, VR / AR technology design, UX / UI, film and content creation, and the mapping out the connected end-to-end persona journeys, all underpinned by deep understanding of cultural, heritage, historical and lifestyle factors and insights that drives our conceptual thinking.
You will be strategic and highly-capable on both B2C and B2B types of project and New Business pitch engagements, experienced at unpacking clients’ briefs to better understand their business, brand and audience challenges, and translate that into creative narrative-led inspiration to guide our design team. 
It is also important that you have the attitude of a hands-on type of creative, able to be ‘on-the-tools’ when needed, to lead by design demonstration, to won presentation decks with a passion for visual excellence. 
You will possess a charisma that shines through with impeccable inter-personal, human and communication skills, confident in articulating our work, presenting to senior clients and liaising with the stakeholders from different departments within our organization. 
As a highly-organized operator, you have proven experience in managing small teams, delegating work-loads, planning foresight, an excellent listener and the ability and maturity to adapt and flex to changing project needs. With an excellent understanding of what’s happening in your core creative industry field, you will have your finger on the pulse of relevant exemplars in the Kingdom and beyond, and will bring these insights and inspiration into our studio.
As a self-starter with an interpleural spirit you will liaise with clients directly in briefings, workshops, brainstorm sessions and presentations, as well as throughout project stages with vendors ensuring projects progress according to intention. 

Please share your portfolio along with your CV , as it is mandatory.

data.title: Senior Manager/Director - Data Lead
data.jd_text:
Please note we are looking to speak to individuals already based in the Riyadh/Dubai as a first priority.

Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consultancy specializing in driving digital transformation in the financial services industry. With a growing client portfolio comprising of over 100 global organizations, Capco operates at the intersection of business and technology by combining innovative thinking with unrivalled industry knowledge to deliver end-to-end data-driven solutions and fast-track digital initiatives for banking and payments, capital markets, wealth and asset management, insurance, and the energy sector. Capco’s cutting-edge ingenuity is brought to life through its Innovation Labs and award-winning Be Yourself At Work culture and diverse talent.

Location: Riyadh/Dubai (On-site) | Practice Area: Data & AI | Type: Permanent

The Role

As a Managing Principal / Director – Data Lead, you’ll play a pivotal role in growing Capco’s Data & Analytics capability in Riyadh. This is a senior, hands-on leadership position that blends delivery ownership with market development. You’ll lead high-impact data programmes for major clients while actively contributing to sales activity, account growth, and the expansion of Capco’s footprint in the region.

What You’ll Do

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex data and analytics engagements, remaining hands-on enough to guide technical direction and outcomesAct as a trusted senior advisor to client stakeholders, translating business objectives into scalable data solutionsDrive business development activity, supporting proposals, shaping client opportunities, and building long-term client relationshipsBuild and grow Capco’s Data & Analytics presence in Riyadh, contributing to capability development and local market strategyMentor and develop teams, fostering an inclusive, high-performing environment aligned to Capco values

What We’re Looking For

Experience in leading Consulting firms10+ years’ practical experience in data, analytics or AI roles with significant, demonstrable exposure to the financial services industrySenior leadership experience delivering large-scale data and analytics programmes within consulting or complex client environmentsStrong technical grounding across data platforms, architecture, and analytics, with the ability to engage credibly in delivery discussionsExcellent stakeholder management skills, with confidence working at executive levelDemonstrable consulting background and experience in delivery or sales, account growth and client development.

Bonus Points For

Experience working in or across Middle Eastern marketsBackground in financial services or adjacent regulated industriesExperience shaping new market offerings or growing regional practicesNative Arabic speaker

Why Join Capco

Deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions for leading organisationsPlay a key role in growing Capco’s Data & AI in the Middle EastWork in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture

Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know – we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration – a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

data.title: Director Talent Development
data.jd_text:
Riyadh Air (RX), headquartered in the Saudi Capital, is the new national airline that’s shaping the future of flying. It seeks to lead the aviation industry by transforming Saudi Arabia into a global aviation and trade hub – a digitally native airline that will connect the kingdom to more than 100 destinations.

About The Role

Are you ready to shape the learning architecture of our digitally native airline and its workforce?

We are seeking a visionary and commercially astute Director of Talent Development to lead our enterprise-wide Learning & Development strategy thereby building a future ready, safety centered and high-performing organisation.

Yours is a role responsible for building a future-ready workforce that strengthens safety, performance, and long-term growth. You are expected to oversee high-impact learning delivery using blended, digital, simulation-based, and experiential methods tailored to aviation.

Partnering closely with senior leaders, you will ensure leadership depth, regulatory compliance, and workforce readiness across all functions.

Importantly, your remit includes integrating leadership development, safety/compliance learning, operational training, and digital capability building into a unified enterprise framework. Further, you will design and implement a fully integrated airline learning ecosystem spanning safety, regulatory, operational, leadership, and digital capabilities.

Central to your success in this role is the ability to own and manage the Talent Development budget, ensuring strategic ROI. Moreover, you will t rack and report KPIs including bench strength, capability readiness, and program effectiveness.

You will also ensure regulatory alignment with key aviation bodies including the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), International Air Transport Association (IATA) and the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA), integrating compliance requirements into enterprise learning frameworks and operational governance.

If you are passionate about building future-ready leaders, strengthening safety culture, and shaping capability at scale, we invite you to lead the next chapter of our talent journey.

About You

Degree qualified, you will demonstrate a minimum of 10 years of sound leadership experience within an L&D or Talent Development capacity interpreting strategy and policy in order to set and deliver objectives within the medium to long term.

We’d love to hear from you even more if you are a bilingual Saudi national with proven leadership experience.

This is your opportunity to shape the future of air travel. Bring your best and show us why you can own this position within Riyadh Air.

Please submit your application by clicking on the Apply button. Our Talent Acquisition team will be reviewing applications shortly.

data.title: Director of Finance
data.jd_text:
The role aims to be recognized as trusted advisors to the business. This role attracts and retains best-in-class finance professionals, develops their finance team and enables them to Thrive. The role communicates with impact to all stakeholders, ensures a robust control and compliance environment and enables the EMEA finance management company value proposition which is attractive to existing and new owners. The role partners with our business to maximize profit for our shareholders and owners and innovates to deliver an industry leading efficient finance organization.

What will I be doing?

The Director of Finance is accountable for the hotel in respect of understanding and driving financial performance and is responsible to perform all finance activities within the policy, regulatory and contractual framework.

The role will business partner with the General Manager and has accountability for developing excellent working relationships across a broad range of internal and external stakeholders, including within Hotel Finance, Commercial, IT, Procurement, Human Resources, Operations, Shared Service Centers (where applicable), Owners and Assett Managers as well as Regional Operations Finance. The Director of Finance works closely with all Business Partners to develop best-in-class hotel Finance teams, including participation in all relevant Finance development programs.

You will be and remain familiar with the management agreement for the Hotel and implement processes and procedures to ensure the Hotel is operated in compliance with the management agreement, reporting issues of non-compliance to your supervising manager.

What are we looking for?

Strategic thinker with the ability to formulate effective goals, objectives and priorities with a long term perspectiveStrong communication and negotiation skills (all levels of management and external customers)Financial and operational analytical skills (operational analysis)Knowledge of departmental and hotel operationsAbility to exercise judgment in evaluating situations and in making sound decisionsAbility to analyze and interpret financial dataLeadership and organizational skills (team orientation, flexible, adaptable)Ability to analyze large volume of complex financial information from many sources and create reports, forecasts projectionsProficiency in MS Office suite applications (Excel, Word, Powerpoint)


Additional Preferences:

University degree in Accounting or Finance / Accounting certification (eg CIMA)Hotel level or industry experience


What will it be like to work for Hilton?

Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full-service hotels and resorts to extended-stay suites and mid-priced hotels. For nearly a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands. Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day. And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!

Work Locations

Conrad Riyadh Laysen Valley

Schedule

Full-time

Brand

Conrad Hotels & Resorts

Job

data.title: Subsurface Director
data.jd_text:
Job PurposeThe Subsurface Director leads all subsurface activities of the company. This includes geology, geophysics, reservoir engineering, petrophysics, geomechanics, and subsurface data. The role covers the full lifecycle—from exploration to field development and production—and ensures safe, efficient, and maximum recovery of resources while supporting the company’s long-term energy strategy.

Strategy & Technical LeadershipDevelop and implement the company’s subsurface strategy.Ensure high-quality technical evaluations of prospects, reservoirs, and field development plans.Set and control subsurface technical standards and workflows.2. Resource & Reserves ManagementLead resources from early concepts through to reserves.Oversee reserves estimation and classification (PRMS or equivalent).Approve reserves booking, production forecasts, and depletion plans.3. Geology & GeophysicsOversee seismic acquisition, processing, and interpretation.Ensure accurate geological and reservoir models.Support exploration, appraisal, and development drilling decisions.4. Reservoir Engineering & Field DevelopmentLead reservoir studies, simulation, and production forecasting.Approve Field Development Plans (FDPs), well locations, and recovery strategies.Ensure continuous monitoring and optimization of reservoirs.5. Production OptimizationWork closely with Operations and Drilling teams to improve production.Support problem-solving for underperforming wells.Ensure long-term reservoir health and integrity.6. Collaboration & Stakeholder ManagementWork with drilling, operations, HSE, commercial, and project teams.Liaise with joint venture partners, regulators, and consultants.Provide subsurface input for business development, M&A, and due diligence.8. Leadership & Team DevelopmentLead and develop a multidisciplinary subsurface team.Build team capability through coaching and succession planning.Promote a strong culture of safety, collaboration, and technical excellence.Key Skills & CompetenciesStrong strategic and analytical thinking.Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.Ability to make decisions under uncertainty.Proven leadership in multicultural and multidisciplinary environments.High integrity and safety-focused mindset.Strong problem-solving abilities.

data.title: Associate Director, WHOOP Labs Doha
data.jd_text:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers its members to perform at a higher level by providing a deep understanding of the interactions between their unique physiology and chosen behaviors.

We’re looking for a talented leader to help open WHOOP Labs Doha, our first international product research facility. The Doha lab will partner with the WHOOP Labs Boston team to conduct mixed-methods research to support physiology, user experience, and consumer studies. To serve the company’s diverse testing and validation needs, WHOOP Labs completes tens of thousands of data collections a year and creates best in class experience for our participants. WHOOP Labs Doha will set the bar for product innovation research in the GCC, setting strategy for participant experience and contributing to advancing WHOOP technology.

We’re looking for someone with a proven track record collaborating internationally and leading a cross-functional team, who is excited to work with our members and isn’t afraid to roll up their sleeves and get sweaty. This position is in-person in Doha, Qatar with potential travel to Boston, MA 1-2 times per year.

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Responsibilities

Own the development and execution of the WHOOP Labs Doha opening strategy, including team hiring, participant compensation structure, and setting operating hoursChampion research integrity, including ensuring that data collection tools and team practices align with local regulations for ethical human subjects researchDefine and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the effectiveness of program initiatives and track progress towards goalsEnsure clear communication between the Boston and Doha labs, developing ways of working that support multi-facility research execution Represent Doha culture and unique participant experience factors in broader strategic conversations with the rest of the WHOOP human subjects research teamBuild the WHOOP Labs Doha brand and represent WHOOP in the GCC region Reports to the Director of Human Subjects Research

Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in a field related to human subjects research such as psychology, neuroscience, exercise physiology, or consumer science5+ years of full-time professional experience in a related area2+ years of success leading a diversely skilled teamExperience working on a distributed international team preferredDeep knowledge of local culture and ability to thoughtfully communicate localization considerations for research at WHOOP Labs DohaUnderstanding of best practices regarding human subjects research, including how to obtain IRB approval for new research projects; research ethics, data privacy, and consent Track record of successful project management, including resource allocation, timeline management, and risk mitigationExcellent verbal and written communication skills. Strong organizational skills, ability to plan ahead while still being flexible and able to work around challengesStrong networking and collaboration skills, with experience in building partnerships and engaging with external stakeholdersAbility to present findings to stakeholders and effectively communicate with company leadersExcitement to work in a high-energy, fast-paced startup environmentUnderstanding of branding and marketing as it applies to research in industry spacesFluency in verbal and written English, WHOOP’s working language

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

data.title: EOI - Project Director (Offshore)
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Wood is accepting expressions of interest for a Project Director (Offshore) to join our team in Qatar. This role create delivery excellence across assigned project/program/portfolio of projects, provide certainty and confidence to the customer/project sponsor, and robust control of project scope, schedule, cost and risk.

Specifies and directs the implementation and completion of one or more major projects or programs, including responsibility for managing safety, scope, cost, schedule and risk, and ensuring appropriate reporting and governance is in place. Responsible for delivery of project(s)/program(s)/portfolio to agreed scope, specifications, schedule and budget, for managing change and customer relationships. Delivers project(s)/program(s)/portfolio which often involves a high level of complexity and risk with multiple workstreams and high financial risk. Oversight of multiple project managers.

Responsibilities

P & L responsibility with associated commercial and financial accountability including revenue delivery, margin and overhead management, budget and forecast determination and attainment, risk and opportunity reviews, cost controls, accounts payable/billing and cash collectionContributes to and reviews the project proposal and plan to establish timeframe, procedures for accomplishing the project, resource requirements, and allotment of available resources to various phases of the project. Ensures adherence to corporate policy and best practice, and sets project evaluation parameters.Negotiates changes to project scope with the customer. Follows Change Management process to identify and approve modifications to scope, cost, schedule and qualityPursues and develop opportunities for innovation, such as digitalisation and technology use, work share and high value engineering centres, to bring value to Wood and customerApproves project procedures and project execution plan including the scope of work, deliverables list, master schedule, budget, org charts, risk register etcEnsures competence of project team members to carry out assigned workOversees proposals and call-offs (may be single projects) within delegation of authority, ensuring compliance with contracting policy and standards of ethical conduct.Monitors adherence to schedule, cost and quality and proactively intervenes to avoid slippageEnsures that the required project management software systems are established for the project and the supporting training is completedEnsure the project assurance protocol and schedule are establishedInvolved in establishing terms and conditions, and wider scope and larger size of projects may require more visibility in the community as well as activity in attracting project staffResponsible for the project HSSE leadership, ensures a program of initiatives and auditing schedule are established, and that HSSE and quality management issues are proactively addressedDrives good practices, including corporate initiatives, across projects and programsResolves complex issues which are escalated by project teamParticipate in assurance activities and peer reviewsThe complexity, potential impact and political sensitivity of projects/programmes of this size (particularly in an international context), frequently require the project director to be involved in closing the deal, attracting specialized staff from outside the organization, and managing relationships with local officials and community groups as well as the customer and contractors, all based on their credibility and proven track record 

Qualifications

data.title: Associate Principal, Business Development - Middle East
data.jd_text:
Who We Are

Welcome to TELUS Digital - where innovation drives impact at a global scale. As an award-winning digital product consultancy and the digital division of TELUS, one of Canada’s largest telecommunications providers, we design and deliver transformative customer experiences through cutting-edge technology, agile thinking, and a people-first culture.

With a global team across North America, South America, Central America, Europe, and APAC, we offer end-to-end expertise across eight core service areas: Digital Product Consulting, Digital Marketing Services, Data & AI, Strategy Consulting, Business Operations Modernization, Enterprise Applications, Cloud Engineering, and QA & Test Engineering.

From mobile apps and websites to voice UI, chatbots, AI, customer service, and in-store solutions, TELUS Digital enables seamless, trusted, and digitally powered experiences that meet customers wherever they are — all backed by the secure infrastructure and scale of our multi-billion-dollar parent company.

Location and Flexibility

The Associate Principal, Business Development is an integral part of our Business Development team at TELUS Digital. This is a hybrid role, located in Dubai, UAE.

The Opportunity 

The Associate Principal will focus on building partnerships and solutions for prospective clients. You’ll interact with a wide variety of businesses, many partners within those businesses, and ever-changing business and technology challenges. We employ a solutions-led strategy and are seeking consultative business development executives, rather than transactional or sales-led business development professionals.

In partnership with our Head of Middle Eastern Operations, the Associate Principal will champion the expansion of our digital services to new client logos, while building and growing our partnerships within the GCC region.

Responsibilities

Act as a core founding team member of the TELUS Digital operation in the Middle East, working closely with the Managing Director to hunt for new logos and develop business Introduce TELUS Digital’s business, proposition, people, and capabilities to new client prospects as we expand within the GCC regionConsult with potential and existing clients to define digital strategy and digital product experiences and develop creative solutions -- help frame business/technical needs, project scopeSource, analyze, prioritize and respond to RFPs, bringing together the best of TELUS Digital practice areas Collaborate closely with different TELUS Digital practices, Solutions Architects and delivery teams, to propose appropriate team/resourcing plans for client briefs and develop pricing proposalsLeverage the talents of the UX Strategy and Design teams to ideate on behalf of clients, develop concepts to pitch, and develop pitch materialsDrive the overall process to combine technical plans and creative concepts into written proposals (RFP, etc) for submission and presentations for pitches, working hand in hand with design teams Identify key accounts and build and maintain relationships with clients to expand partnerships and extend services on a client-by-client basisAssist with developing strategies and plans for developing and deepening accounts Some travel required for client visits, pitches, and conferences

Competencies 

Minimum of 5 years in a top tier management consulting firm with experience in digital or technology environment, delivering large scale technology transformationExperience working with, and presenting to, C-level professionals of Fortune 500 companiesProven success in project leadership roles with ability to translate technical concepts into business objectivesSkilled problem solver and value driver for clients. Ability to move clients toward valuable recommendations and high-impact solutionsA deep sense of ownership and genuine enthusiasm for coming up with solutions for brands/clientsEngineering or computer science degree and/or background preferredProficiency in both English (required) and Modern Standard Arabic (preferred)Hustle, hunger, and drive to build and grow a business Hands-on and willing to roll up your sleeves and take end to end ownership of activities with minimal supervision Able to see the big picture and get in the weeds as needed Collaborative and able to work across multiple work zones and wear multiple hats

Equal Opportunity Employer

At TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicants’ qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.

We will only use the information you provide to process your application and to produce tracking statistics. Since we do not request personal data deemed sensitive, we ask you to abstain from sharing that information with us.

data.title: Steel Structure QC Inspector (UAE National)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a Steel Structure QC Inspector, reporting directly to the Head of the Department to join our team based in UAE, Abu Dhabi.

About us:

Technip Energies is a global technology and engineering powerhouse. With leadership positions in LNG, hydrogen, ethylene, sustainable chemistry, and CO2 management, we are contributing to the development of critical markets such as energy, energy derivatives, decarbonization, and circularity. Our complementary business segments, Technology, Products and Services (TPS) and Project Delivery, turn innovation into scalable and industrial reality.

Through collaboration and excellence in execution, our 17,000+ employees across 34 countries are fully committed to bridging prosperity with sustainability for a world designed to last.

About the mission we offer you:

Location: Ruwais, UAE

Experience: 6 months – 1 year

Nationality: UAE Nationals only

Employment Type: Full-time – Site-based

Key Responsibilities

Assist in conducting quality inspections for steel structure fabrication and installation activities.Verify that steel works comply with approved drawings, project specifications, and international standards.Monitor welding activities, fit-up, alignment, bolting, and material traceability on site.Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, and QC teams to resolve quality-related issues.Support inspection activities such as dimensional checks, visual welding inspections, and bolt torque verification.Prepare and maintain daily QC reports, inspection records, and non-conformance documentation.Report deviations, defects, or non-compliance to the QC Engineer/Supervisor promptly.Ensure adherence to HSE and quality procedures while promoting safe working practices.


Qualifications & Requirements

UAE National (Emiratisation role).6 months to 1 year of experience in steel structure QC, welding inspection, or related field.Diploma or bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, or a related discipline (preferred).Basic understanding of steel structure drawings, welding processes, and inspection techniques.Knowledge of QA/QC procedures and documentation practices.Strong communication and teamwork skills.Willingness to work in Ruwais on a site-based schedule.


Skills & Competencies

Basic knowledge of steel structure fabrication and erection.Attention to detail and strong observation skills.Ability to work in a fast-paced, construction-driven environment.Commitment to safety, quality, and compliance standards.


Starting Date: ASAP

Your career with us 

Working at Technip Energies is an inspiring journey, filled with groundbreaking projects and dynamic collaborations. Surrounded by diverse and talented individuals, you will feel welcomed, respected, and engaged. Enjoy a safe, caring environment where you can spark new ideas, reimagine the future, and lead change. As your career grows, you will benefit from learning opportunities at T. EN University, such as The Future Ready Program, Graduate Program, and from the support of your manager through check-in moments like the Mid-Year Development Review, fostering continuous growth and development.

What’s Next?

Once receiving your system application, our recruiting team will screen and match your skills, experience, and potential team fit against the role requirements. We ask for your patience as the team completes the volume of applications with a reasonable timeframe. Check your application progress periodically via personal account from created candidate profile during your application.

We invite you to get to know more about our company by visiting www.ten.com and follow us on LinkedIn , Instagram , Facebook , X and YouTube for company updates.

data.title: Senior Principal Engineer - Satellite Platform
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
FADA is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Satellite Platform Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this role, the candidate will be responsible for leading the design, integration, and validation of satellite bus systems for advanced space missions. He will work closely with cross-functional engineering teams to ensure the successful development of the satellites.

The successful candidate will be responsible for developing high-performance platform accommodating advanced payloads for satellite systems, localising technologies and knowhow, and managing project timelines, budgets, resources, and risk, and will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery.

The Satellite Platform Engineer will report to the VP of Engineering and Capability Development and will work closely with the System Engineer and the Payload Specialists.

Responsibilities:

 Lead end-to-end system engineering activities for satellite platforms, including requirements definition, architecture design, subsystem integration, and performance analysis.  Oversee technical coordination across propulsion, power, thermal, avionics, and structural subsystems to ensure platform-level compatibility and mission success.  Manage interface control documents (ICDs), system budgets (mass, power, data), and verification plans in alignment with mission objectives and customer requirements.  Conduct trade studies, risk assessments, and design reviews (SRR, PDR, CDR) to support decision-making and ensure compliance with space standards.  Collaborate with cross-functional teams including payload engineers, mission planners, and ground segment specialists to ensure seamless system integration.  Work closely with subject matter experts for payloads to ensure platform design supports payload requirements and operational constraints.  Support launch and early orbit operations (LEOP), anomaly resolution, and post-launch performance evaluations.  Mentor junior engineers and contribute to continuous improvement of engineering processes and tools.  Support business development activities by providing technical expertise during customer engagements and strategic planning.  Contribute to technical proposals, including system architecture descriptions, compliance matrices, and cost estimates.  Contribute to and support R&D activities related to satellite platform technologies, including advanced materials, subsystem innovations, and new mission concepts. 

Experience:

 10+ years of experience in satellite system engineering, preferably with flight-proven platforms.  Proven track record of leading successful satellite projects from concept to deployment.  Extensive experience with satellite design, integration, and testing.  Strong background in systems engineering principles and practices.  Familiarity with industry standards and regulatory requirements for satellite systems. 

Qualifications

 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Systems Engineering, or related field.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.  Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.  Proficiency in relevant software tools and programming languages.  Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.  Strong leadership and project management skills. 

#FADA

data.title: Senior Solution Architect (Databricks & Energy Sector Architecture)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Talan is a global consulting group specialising in innovation and transformation through technology. For over 20 years, we have partnered with companies and public organisations to drive meaningful change.

We are purpose driven, working across diverse sectors to tackle social and environmental challenges, simplify and improve market operations, leverage digital transformation, and ensure that data is managed responsibly and ethically.

Our expertise spans consultancy and outsourcing services, including Technology, Data & AI and Consulting

Senior Solution Architect (Databricks & Energy Sector Architecture)

Job Description

Role Overview

The Senior Solution Architect will lead the design, assessment, and governance of data and analytics platforms — with a strong focus on Databricks — for energy utilities operating within the GCC and UAE. The role requires deep technical and data leadership, domain expertise in electricity and water utilities, and the ability to shape enterprise-scale data architectures aligned with regulatory, cybersecurity, and operational requirements unique to the UAE energy landscape.

This position supports strategy, architecture assessment, creation or improvement of data platforms, vendor engagement, and end‑to‑end solution assurance across large‑scale digital and data transformation programmes.

Key Responsibilities

1. Architecture Leadership & Design


Define and own end‑to‑end data and analytics architecture using Databricks, Delta Lake, ML/AI workloads, and real‑time data processing pipelines. Lead the integration of modern cloud-native platforms with legacy OT/IT systemsDesign architectural blueprints for data use cases such as demand and production forecast, AI based data analysis, grid analytics, power network balance, - experience of similar frameworks applied for energy & utility companies within the GCC for digital transformation programmes would be a significant advantageEnsure cross-platform interoperability across cloud solutions, data platforms, integration middleware, smart metering, net metering or IoT platforms, and cybersecurity baselines (e.g., NESA, TRA, energy regulators etc). 


2. Solution Architecture Assessments


Conduct architecture maturity assessments, platform capability reviews, and gap analysis for data, integration, and operational systems. Evaluate vendor architectures, technical documentation, and compliance with GCC & UAE energy standards. Prepare architecture assurance reports, risks & mitigation strategies, and recommendations for platform consolidation. 


3. Data Platform Engineering & Governance


Oversee design patterns for ingestion, ETL/ELT, orchestration, model training, governance, and data quality frameworks on Databricks. Establish data governance, RACI models, and minimum-security baselines for data and IT/OT systems — consistent with global industry best practices. Ensure compliance with local cybersecurity frameworks, local data protection laws, and critical infrastructure requirements. 


4. Stakeholder, Vendor & Regulatory Engagement


Lead Participate in technical workshops with various stakeholders (internal, vendors, regulators) - echoing responsibilities seen across energy & utilities consultancy roles. Translate business objectives into data-driven architectural strategies for multi‑utility environments (electricity/water/cooling). Support RFP/RFQ evaluations, vendor scorecards, integration assessments, and architecture due diligence. 


5. Delivery & Technical Assurance


Participate in delivery teams, ensuring architectural and data-oriented decisions are implemented consistently. Manage PoCs, pilots, cloud deployments, and scaling of enterprise data platforms. Provide and implement technical advice for migration from legacy environments into unified data analytics ecosystems. 


 





Qualifications

Required Technical Skills & Experience

Databricks & Cloud Technologies


5–10 years experience in designing and implementing data platformsProven experience in data management with Databricks (Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, SQL/ML workloads) and similar platforms. Expert in distributed compute, Spark optimisation, workflow orchestration (Jobs, Delta Live Tables). Strong hands-on experience with Azure (Data Factory, Synapse, ADLS, Key Vault, DevOps, networking) and AWS


Energy Sector Data Architecture


Demonstrated experience designing or assessing data management systems for:Smart metering ecosystems (MDMS, HES, concentrators, IoT)Grid analytics, balance management, forecasting, outage management, customer systems

Understanding of GCC & UAE regulatory frameworks energy-market constraints. 


Integration & Enterprise Architecture


Strong knowledge of system integration patterns, API management, middleware, and enterprise platform interoperability. Experience modernising legacy systems


Cybersecurity & Governance


Familiarity with UAE NESA standards, OT/IT security approaches, risk-based governance, and minimum-security b

data.title: QNB3555 - Senior Vice President - Finance (Saudization)
data.jd_text:
About QNB

Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.

QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries across three continents providing a comprehensive range of advanced products and services. The total number of employees is more than 28,000 serving up to 20 million customers operating through 1,000 locations, with an ATM network of 4,300 machines.

QNB has maintained its position as one of the highest rated regional banks from leading credit rating agencies including Standard & Poor’s (A), Moody’s (Aa3) and Fitch (A+). The Bank has also been the recipient of many awards from leading international specialised financial publications.

Based on the Group’s consistent strong financial performance and its expanding international presence, QNB currently ranks as the most valuable bank brand in the Middle East and Africa, according to Brand Finance Magazine.

QNB Group has an active community support program and sponsors various social, educational and sporting events.

Job Summary

The Senior Vice President (SVP) will manage all aspects of finance of the local operations and provide support to the Head Office- International Finance Department in all areas of Bank’s business financials in the assigned location. The SVP is one of the primary points of contact with the regulatory authorities and the external and internal auditors and is key in ensuring good relationships with such bodies. Responsible for managing the local tax framework of the bank. The SVP ensures good corporate governance is applied throughout the organization. Applies insight and understanding to the financial performance of the banks business in that country and contributes to the strategic and day to day commercial decision making process.

Main Responsibilities

 Shareholder & Financial: Ensure timely and accurate information as well as disclosure of all material matters concerning the operations. Develop mechanisms designed to ensure compliance with all relevant local laws and regulations and also international laws and regulations. Act within the limits of the powers delegated to the incumbent and monitor that respective heads exercise the same in respect to the Financial Delegation of Authority. Assist Country CEO in developing/ creating the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), for performance monitoring and quality measurement purposes pertaining to the financial reporting related process and monitor the achievements of its individual departments/ units on periodic basis. Provides leadership on creating Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for the departments under the incumbent’s

supervision for performance monitoring purposes

 Observes and promotes cost consciousness and efficiency, and enhance productivity, to minimize costs, avoid wastes, and optimize benefits for the bank Customer (Internal/External): Establishes and enhance relationships with regulatory authorities to ensure that all regulatory reporting requirements are compliant. Establishes and enhance relationships with tax authorities to ensure that all statutory requirements are compliant. Liaises with external auditors on matters pertaining to presentation and disclosure of financial statements, emerging financial reporting trends, impacts of proposed accounting standards etc. To assist customers in all their queries on Bank’s product and seek solution to their requests Maintain activities in accordance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in turn-around time. Build and maintain strong and effective relationship with all other related departments and units to achieve the

Group’s goals/objectives.

 Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal Auditors, Compliance, Financial Control and Risk Functions as and when required Internal (Processes, Products, Regulatory): Ensures maintenance of general accounting, cost accounting, payroll, payables, and receivables. Ensures accurate and timely recording and reporting of operating results. Provides financial analysis, interpretation of variances from budgets and standards, and advice to management. Sets up, documents and reviews Financial Control procedures. Controls supplier payments and ensures that the payments are made in time and proper administration of the Administration System. Ensures accurate reconciliation of accounts to give operational support to all areas and minimizes the costs of maintaining the accounts.

Ensures monthly/quarterly/half yearly returns submitted in time and action taken when required.

 Responsible for Annual Budget for the allocated Country and monitoring of variances to actual vs budget, throughout the year. Maintain activities in accordance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in tu

data.title: QNB3552 - Vice President – Corporate Relationship Management (Saudization)
data.jd_text:
About QNB

Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.

QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries across three continents providing a comprehensive range of advanced products and services. The total number of employees is more than 28,000 serving up to 20 million customers operating through 1,000 locations, with an ATM network of 4,300 machines.

QNB has maintained its position as one of the highest rated regional banks from leading credit rating agencies including Standard & Poor’s (A), Moody’s (Aa3) and Fitch (A+). The Bank has also been the recipient of many awards from leading international specialised financial publications.

Based on the Group’s consistent strong financial performance and its expanding international presence, QNB currently ranks as the most valuable bank brand in the Middle East and Africa, according to Brand Finance Magazine.

QNB Group has an active community support program and sponsors various social, educational and sporting events.

Job Summary

The incumbent will be primarily responsible for managing on a day-to-day basis a portfolio of existing and potential Senior/large corporate customers to meet assigned financial targets of assigned country location. Incumbent is expected to develop and profitably manage banking relationships for business customers within their geographic territory or industry/customer portfolio. This position functions as the primary resource for extension of the full range of bank products and services applicable to the business customer. These will typically include credit and deposit services, as well as international transactions or other specialized services.

Main Responsibilities

 Shareholder & Financial: Play a major role in the implementation of the strategies and plan to achieve all set volume, revenue and profit targets for the Corporate Banking function within the assigned country location and in accordance with QNB’s overall corporate budget and strategy. Come up consistently with business ideas to help boost revenues Develop Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for the purpose of performance monitoring and quality measurement of the Corporate Banking Unit and monitor their achievements on periodic basis. Observes and promotes cost consciousness and efficiency, and enhance productivity, to minimize costs, avoid wastes, and optimize benefits for the bank. Act within the limits of the powers delegated to the incumbent and adheres to QNB policies. Delegate’s authority to the respective staff and monitor exercise of the same. Customer (Internal & External): Develop and manage on a day-to-day basis potential corporate customers and assigned portfolio of existing customers to meet assigned financial and non-financial targets in compliance with established QNB credit policies, procedures and standards. Build and maintain strong and effective relationships with all related functions / units to achieve assigned goals / objectives. Responsible for the day to day management of the Relationship Manager (RM) and Assistant Relationship Manager (ARM). Ensure appropriate direction of work and duties and that RM and ARM has relevant training and support. Responsible for business development and origination as well as credit recommendations in favour of selected local and regional corporations Provide timely and accurate information to the Head of Corporate & Institutional Banking and head office regarding credit and compliance issues in local and regional markets Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal auditors when required. To assist customers in all their queries on Bank’s product and seek solution to their requests. Maintain activities in accordance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/units to achieve improvements in turn-around time. Provide timely/accurate data to external/internal Auditors, Compliance, Financial Control and Risk when required. Internal (Processes, Products, Regulatory): Prepare comprehensive credit proposals for new/prospect customers that adequately address all relevant risk factors and satisfy minimum acceptable standards of CPM. At least one month before expiry, prepare comprehensive credit review for existing portfolio's that adequately address all relevant and current risk factors and the latest developments in the industry, market and business. Stress and document periodic calling on customers to capture ongoing businesses and encourage reliance on QNB. Stress account management to enhance utilization of lines. Restrict excess/ casual facilities and maintain the relationship within the approved boundaries. Identify and timely report on all negative events and early signals on potential problematic accounts/ relationships. Obtain all requisite facility and account documentation to ensure ea

data.title: Chief Strategy Officer
data.jd_text:
Our client is a leading financial services Group in the GCC seeking to appoint a Chief Strategy Officer. This is a strictly confidential mandate for a senior executive who will shape Group wide strategy, enterprise alignment, and long-term value creation.
This role carries direct exposure to the CEO and Board, with responsibility for defining strategic direction, evaluating growth opportunities, and ensuring disciplined execution across a diversified financial platform.
The successful candidate will combine macro level strategic vision with structured execution capability, ensuring the Group remains competitive, resilient, and growth focused across regional and international markets.
Key Responsibilities:
Define, refine, and oversee execution of the Group wide corporate strategy aligned with long term growth and shareholder objectivesAdvise the CEO and Board on strategic priorities, market positioning, and enterprise transformation initiativesLead the evaluation, vetting, and development of strategic initiatives including expansion, partnerships, and corporate development opportunitiesEstablish and maintain robust strategy management frameworks, policies, and performance monitoring mechanismsDrive cross functional alignment across business units to ensure cohesive and disciplined execution of strategic objectivesOversee business intelligence activities including market analysis, competitive bench marking, and identification of emerging opportunities across regulated and non regulated segmentsSupport Board level reporting on strategic progress, risk considerations, and forward-looking initiatives
Key Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline. MBA or equivalent postgraduate qualification preferred15+ years of progressive experience within financial services, diversified financial groups, sovereign related entities, or leading strategy consulting firmsDemonstrated experience in enterprise strategy formulation and execution within complex financial institutionsStrong exposure to corporate development, transformation initiatives, and performance management frameworksProven ability to influence and operate effectively at CEO and Board levelDeep understanding of GCC financial services landscapes and regulatory environmentsExceptional analytical capability, stakeholder management skills, and executive presence
This appointment is being managed on a confidential basis. Further information will be shared with shortlisted candidates following initial discussion.
If interested, please apply directly or send an email to daniel.brocklebank@madisonpearl.com for a discreet conversation.

*Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted*

data.title: Vice President of Product Engineering, 2 Hour Learning (Remote) - $400,000/year USD
data.jd_text:
Are you a technical product leader with deep engineering expertise who is ready to spearhead the reinvention of education through artificial intelligence? 2 Hour Learning seeks a technical architect to drive the modernization of our AI-powered learning ecosystem. This position requires someone with the technical discipline to function as a CTO, the product vision to lead transformational initiatives, and the commitment to apply these capabilities toward unlocking student potential at scale.

Traditional education companies add AI to existing models as an add-on. 2 Hour Learning has taken a fundamentally different approach: we have reconstructed the school model from first principles, leveraging AI and learning science to eliminate the limitations of the conventional classroom. Our students do not simply improve — they excel, earning multiple 4s and 5s on AP exams, demonstrating multi-year academic gains within a single year, and routinely exceeding their own projections. We have validated that when AI serves as the foundational operating system for learning, student performance surges.

This is not a conventional product management position centered on customer feedback cycles or incremental feature releases. This is an executive-level technical product ownership role, where you will own the architecture, technical strategy, and AI integration across our entire learning platform. You will be accountable for making critical technical tradeoffs that keep our products lean, scalable, and unwaveringly focused on advancing student outcomes. You will think with the rigor of a CTO and execute with the agility of a product entrepreneur, combining engineering excellence with learning science principles to drive continuous innovation.

If you are prepared to lead the technical transformation of education and possess the executional discipline to support that vision, we invite you to apply. This is your chance to shape the future of learning.

What You Will Be Doing

Building and maintaining deep expertise in education products to inform strategic directionCreating technical specifications that establish key technical decisions and emphasize simplicity, efficiency, and sustainabilityDeploying generative AI technologies to automate and optimize engineering workflows, in alignment with the strategic objective of minimizing manual effort

What You Won’t Be Doing

Exclusively managing people; your technical and business judgment will be central to this roleSpending time on meetings, committees, and administrative overhead; we have automated management processes to allow you to concentrate on delivering business value through direct contributionProposing high-level strategies without actionable implementation paths; your guidance must be grounded in product-level understanding

Vice President Of Product Engineering Key Responsibilities

Serve as the CTO for one or more education products, owning core capabilities and generating business value through flawless execution and strategic leadership

Basic Requirements

7+ years of experience making critical architecture and design decisions on software projects, including the application of architecture design patterns or major open source technologies4+ years of experience in roles with complete technical and delivery ownership of a revenue-generating productExperience leveraging AI agents to design, implement, and test software products or features

About 2 Hour Learning

Education is broken, but 2 Hour Learning is proving it doesn’t have to be. They’re tearing down the outdated one-size-fits-all model and replacing it with AI-driven personalized learning that helps kids master academics in just two hours a day.

With students consistently ranking in the top 1-2% nationally and the top 20% achieving an astonishing 6.5x growth, they’re proving that smarter learning is possible. At 2 Hour Learning, it’s talent and performance that matter. 

They offer a dynamic, on-campus and remote-friendly environment where innovators, educators, and AI specialists can be a part of fixing a broken school system.

2 Hour Learning is reprogramming learning for the AI era.

Here’s How They’re Fixing It.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

This is a full-time (40 hours per week), long-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $200 USD/hour, which equates to $400,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help-and-faqs for more details on this topic.

Crossover Job Code: LJ-5590-AE-Dubai-VicePresidento.024

data.title: General Manager and Senior Vice President, Operations
data.jd_text:
( 19445 )

A part of Kerzner International, a global leader in luxury hospitality, Atlantis, The Palm is a world where iconic stays, legendary adventures, and the boldest flavours all come together to transport guests to A World Away.

The iconic resort, located on the crescent of Palm Island, features 1,544 Rooms, Suites, and Signature Suites, each designed with thoughtful details inspired by the sea. It is the ultimate destination for world-class dining, bringing together some of the most renowned celebrity chefs and Michelin-starred restaurants. The resort is also home to unforgettable experiences, from record-breaking thrills at the world’s largest waterpark to stunning views of 65,000 marine animals in the aquarium, and close encounters with resident Indo-Pacific dolphins at Dolphin Bay.

Behind this extraordinary destination are over 6,000 colleagues from 129 nationalities. Together, we foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where talent is nurtured, wellbeing is prioritised, and careers truly thrive.

The General Manager & SVP, Operations oversees all hotel operations, to achieve planned operational goals and profitability objectives, including but not limited to revenues, operation profits, while maintaining company standards for guest satisfaction, colleague engagement, quality assurance, and asset protection. The General Manager & SVP, Operations will demonstrate leadership and a professional image to colleagues, guests, clients, local community representatives, vendors, and competitors.

 Key Responsibilities 

 Hold full accountability for the overall operational and financial performance of the hotel. Lead and oversee all departments including Rooms, Food and Beverage, Sales and Marketing, Revenue, Finance, Facilities, IT and Human Resources across pre opening and operational phases. Drive revenue growth, operational efficiency and profitability through disciplined financial management, forecasting and execution of the annual business plan. Monitor key performance drivers including sales, guest satisfaction, colleague engagement, quality assurance and asset protection. Align operations, management style and decision making with corporate vision, values, standards and governance requirements. Build strong relationships with the owning company and maintain transparent communication on performance and strategic priorities. Provide clear direction to the Executive Committee, promote open dialogue and foster a culture of accountability, collaboration and high standards. Inspire colleagues to deliver personalised and instinctive luxury guest experiences. Attract, retain and develop high potential talent while creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and innovation. Ensure compliance with all government regulations, health and safety requirements, emergency procedures and corporate programmes. Safeguard hotel assets through capital planning, maintenance oversight and long term asset protection strategies. Represent the hotel in key sales activities, stakeholder engagements and community initiatives.

Skills, Experience & Educational Requirements

 Bachelor’s Degree required, preferably in Hotel, Restaurant or Business Administration, or equivalent experience. Minimum 10 years general management experience in a senior corporate hospitality role preferred. Proven experience in luxury resort environments with strong understanding of Forbes five star and Michelin standards. Experience managing large scale resorts exceeding 500 rooms. Experience with luxury pre openings preferred. Experience managing branded residences preferred. Strong financial acumen and commercial awareness. Excellent oral, report writing and presentation skills. Working knowledge of large enterprise systems. High level of discretion and professionalism. Strong problem solving and decision making capability. Able to challenge constructively and influence through sound reasoning. Pragmatic, adaptable and comfortable leading change. Ability to relocate.

We offer a competitive, tax-free salary paid in UAE Dirhams (AED), alongside a wealth of benefits. These include generous food and beverage privileges, exclusive discounted rates at all Kerzner properties worldwide, comprehensive health insurance (with dental coverage), annual flights home, life insurance, an incentive program, and a variety of colleague wellness and social initiatives.

With a strong emphasis on learning and development, colleagues at Atlantis enjoy access to world-class training, career mobility, and internal growth opportunities across the Kerzner portfolio - making this an ideal environment for ambitious individuals seeking a long-term, fulfilling career in luxury hospitality. Join the A-Team and discover the extraordinary.
