data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text:
Location: Dubai, UAE (with absolute fluency in French)Reports to: CEO
ALPHA10X is an award-winning, AI-native Decision Intelligence platform built for private-market underwriting. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Dubai with applied research in France, the company has raised ~$20M in seed from prominent investors, technology-sector operators, and leading scientists—forming an exceptional cap table. With $2.3M in ARR, it is moving towards raising a Series A to scale R&D and go-to-market, with the primary build-out centered in the Middle East. The business requires institutional-grade financial leadership to support operating discipline, capital formation, and long-term value creation.
The RoleThe CFO will build and lead the finance function of ALPHA10X at a critical stage of growth. This is a hands-on and strategic role for a high-calibre finance leader who combines strong accounting and controlling fundamentals with private markets capital raise credibility, planning discipline, and commercial judgment. The CFO will own the financial architecture of the business: reporting, controls, forecasting, liquidity, investor readiness, capital raise support, and financial decision-making. The role requires someone capable of operating in a fast-moving, multinational environment with high standards of precision, pace, and communication.
Core MandateEstablish robust accounting, controlling, and reporting infrastructure.Lead financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and modelling.Manage cash, runway, and capital efficiency.Support the CEO on fundraising, investor materials, due diligence, and financing strategy.Produce clear, decision-grade financial analysis for leadership and the Board.Ensure the company is financially and operationally prepared to scale internationally.
Key ResponsibilitiesAccounting, Reporting, and ControllingBuild and oversee a rigorous finance / accounting function across a multinational structure.Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual close.Own financial reporting, reconciliations, controls, and management accounts.Coordinate external accountants, auditors, tax advisors, banks, and legal counsel.Ensure compliance across jurisdictions and maintain audit readiness.
FP&A, Business Planning, and ModellingLead budgeting, forecasting, and long-range financial planning.Develop and maintain operating and financial models with scenario analysis.Build KPI frameworks and performance dashboards across the business.Translate strategy into operating plans and capital requirements.Provide financial analysis to support decision-making.
Capital Raise and Investor ReadinessSupport the CEO in executing equity and debt financing processes.Prepare investor reporting, board materials, and diligence documentation.Own the financial data room and ensure alignment of numbers and assumptions.Support capital structure analysis and financing strategy.
Cash Flow and Financial DisciplineMaintain oversight of liquidity, runway, and working capital.Improve financial visibility and operating discipline across the company.Ensure robust forecasting accuracy and spend discipline.
Candidate Profile5—10 years of senior finance experience.Strong background in accounting, financial control, and reporting.Strong experience in FP&A, business planning, forecasting, and modelling.Experience operating in a multi-entity, multinational environment.Experience supporting or leading capital raises and investor reporting.Professional qualification such as ACA, ACCA, CIMA, CPA, or CA preferred.
Skills and Personal AttributesExceptional command of written and spoken French (in addition to English).Strong executive presence with investors and board members.High analytical capability and attention to detail.Strong commercial judgment and strategic thinking.Ability to operate in a fast-paced entrepreneurial environment.
What success looks likeFinance infrastructure that is accurate, scalable, and investor-grade.Timely and credible financial reporting.Robust financial models and planning processes supporting management decisions.Strong investor readiness and capital formation capability.Improved financial visibility, discipline, and governance.

data.title: Senior Manager-Business Excellence
data.jd_text:
Job Description

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 


Lead a portfolio of high-impact Business Excellence and Process Transformation initiatives aligned with enterprise strategy. Build strong, trusted relationships with senior stakeholders to ensure alignment, sponsorship, and adoption of improvement programs. Drive deployment, governance, and continuous enhancement of the Enterprise Process Excellence Framework, ensuring sustainability of outcomes. Ensure realization of measurable business benefits (financial, process, customer and compliance). Lead scoping, planning, and execution of continuous improvement initiatives, process reengineering, and cross‑functional transformation projects. Conduct and facilitate end‑to‑end process mapping, root-cause analysis, redesign, and optimization using best‑practice methodologies. Develop and maintain enterprise process documentation, including policies, SOPs, KPIs, RACI, controls framework, and training materials. Maintain and govern the enterprise process library through BPM tools (e.g., ARIS), ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely updates. Evaluate existing (“As‑Is”) processes, identify gaps, and design optimized (“To‑Be”) workflows that drive efficiency, compliance, and digital enablement. Contribute to enterprise transformation initiatives such as automation, digitization, operational excellence programs, and new business model implementations.


MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:


 Bachelor’s degree in Business Management, Engineering, Finance, or related field.  Minimum 12-15 years’ experience in Business Excellence, Process Transformation, or Operational Excellence, within Real Estate or large diversified groups focusing on Real Estate.  Demonstrated expertise in driving process excellence and operational transformation within Real Estate Development or Real Estate focused consulting engagements.  Strong exposure to Intelligent Automation in a process re-engineering context, including AI Driven Automation, experience with process mining/process intelligence platforms (e.g. Celonis, Signavio, Power Automate, etc.).  Experience in digital transformation, automation opportunities (RPA/BPM), and operating model redesign.  Strong expertise in Business Process Management methodologies and frameworks; experience with industry-leading BPM tools (preferably ARIS).  Project Management Professional (PMP) and Six Sigma Black Belt certifications.  Proven experience leading complex, cross‑functional projects from concept to execution with successful benefit realization.  Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and conceptual thinking skills with ability to introduce creative and effective solutions.

data.title: Compliance Senior Manager - Digital Assets
data.jd_text:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

Wise is looking for an experienced and forward-thinking Compliance Senior Manager to join our team in Dubai. This role will be responsible for building, leading, and managing the compliance framework for our new stablecoin and virtual asset services, licensed by Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA). This is a unique opportunity to shape our compliance strategy from the ground up under a new regulatory paradigm.

As the Compliance Senior Manager, you will be the primary point of contact with VARA, ensuring that our operations not only meet but exceed regulatory expectations. You will work closely with a global team of specialists across Product, Legal, Operations, and Engineering to launch and scale innovative products safely and compliantly, helping us achieve our mission of making money instant, convenient, and transparent for everyone.

Key Responsibilities

 Regulatory Oversight & LicensingAct as the designated Virtual Asset Compliance Officer (VACO) as required by Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA), holding ultimate responsibility for the integrity and effectiveness of the compliance framework.Own the relationship with VARA, serving as the point of contact for all licensing, reporting, examinations, and strategic engagement.Build and maintain a robust, scalable compliance program tailored to the VARA framework.Oversee the maintenance of our VARA license, monitor, interpret, and advise the business on all regulatory developments from VARA and other relevant UAE authorities. Governance & AdvisoryLocalise and implement Wise’s global compliance policies to ensure they are fit-for-purpose and compliant with VARA rules.Develop and deliver targeted training to the Board and senior management on the VARA framework and the specific risks associated with virtual assets.Report directly to the Board and senior leadership on the health of the compliance program, providing clear insights into emerging risks and the effectiveness of controls.Act as the key strategic advisor to the business, providing clear and practical compliance guidance on new product development and market expansion. Financial Crime ComplianceDesign and manage the financial crime risk management framework, addressing risks specific to virtual assets such as blockchain analytics, the "Travel Rule", and counter-party VASP due diligence.Own the Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR/STR) process, managing interactions with the UAE’s Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU).Establish risk-based procedures for customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD), and act as the point of escalation for high-risk customer approvals.Regularly assess the money laundering, terrorist financing, and sanctions risks associated with our virtual asset products and services.Implement systems and key risk indicators (KRIs) to track and monitor our regulatory and financial crime risks effectively.

This Role Will Give You The Opportunity To

Build a compliance function from the ground up in the pioneering regulatory landscape of virtual assets in Dubai.Leverage your subject matter expertise to shape and influence the application of the VARA framework, setting a new standard for compliance in the virtual assets sector.Collaborate with brilliant teams across Wise, embedding compliance in our product innovation and growth strategy.

Qualifications & Experience

Minimum of 8 years’ experience in a regulatory or financial crime compliance role, OR 5 years of experience combined with a relevant professional certification (e.g., Certified Cryptoasset Financial Crime Compliance (CCFCC), Chainalysis Reactor Certification (CRC)).Demonstrable, direct, hands-on experience and in-depth knowledge of the virtual assets/cryptocurrency sector, including an understanding of key risks and industry best practices.Profound knowledge of UAE AML/CFT regulations and the FATF Recommendations for Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs). Experience with the VARA framework is a significant advantage.Proven ability to build and manage a compliance program, with experience in policy writing, risk assessment, and regulatory engagement.A "growth mindset" – you are passionate about expanding Wise's mission in a compliant, pragmatic, and solution-oriented manner.Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and build trust with internal and external stakeholders.Ability to work autonomously in a fast-

data.title: Financial Partnership Development Senior Manager
data.jd_text:
About Thunes

Thunes is the Smart Superhighway for money movement around the world. Thunes’ proprietary Direct Global Network allows Members to make payments in real-time in over 130 countries and more than 80 currencies.

Thunes’ Network connects directly to over 7 billion mobile wallets and bank accounts worldwide, via more than 350 different payment methods, such as GCash, M-Pesa, Airtel, MTN, Orange, JazzCash, Easypaisa, AliPay, WeChat Pay and many more.

Members of Thunes’ Direct Global Network include gig economy giants like Uber and Deliveroo, super-apps like Grab and WeChat, MTOs, fintechs, PSPs and banks. Thunes’ Direct Global Network differentiates itself through its worldwide reach, in-house Smart Treasury Management Platform and Fortress Compliance Infrastructure, ensuring Members of the Network receive unrivalled speed, control, visibility, protection and cost efficiencies when making real-time payments globally.

Headquartered in Singapore, Thunes has offices in 12 locations, including Barcelona, Beijing, Dubai, London, Manila, Nairobi, Paris, Riyadh, San Francisco, Sao Paulo and Shanghai. For more information, visit: https://www.thunes.com/

Context of the role

Thunes is looking for a dynamic, high impact leader to drive the growth, health, and innovation of our Direct Global Network across MENA. The Network Development Manager is technically and financially savvy, creative, with strong personal vision, extremely strong domain expertise, and great people skills.

Key Responsibilities

As a key member of the Network team, you will be asked to wear many hats, including developing country and regional go-to-market strategies, cultivating network partners at all levels of the payments and financial services ecosystem, and developing a well-functioning payments network in MENA that drives volume across our global network, including:

Strategic partnership management & execution: Strategically plan, execute, and manage end-to-end relationships with critical banking and network partners (FIs). This includes strategic road map development in the region and managing key accountsCommercial growth & network competitiveness: Continually drive revenue through cross and up-sell opportunities, in close collaboration with Go-To-Market and Account Management teams. Ensure the Thunes network is the most commercially attractive in the market, from transaction cost to FX, and work with FI partners to drive payment flows through joint go-to-market initiatives.Operational excellence & stakeholder coordination: Act as an owner to ensure all aspects of partnerships are working seamlessly. Work with internal and external stakeholders (including Risk, Compliance, Product, Engineering, and Treasury) to ensure the Thunes network is operationally excellent, coordinate on partner strategy, and advance a holistic network strategy.Market knowledge: Understand and clearly articulate the payment-related nuances among the different markets in-scope (e.g. UAE vs Jordan etc.


Professional Experience/Qualifications

Must have a minimum of 3+ years in a payments scheme, Network Management, or related roleMust be able to ‘talk shop’ with experienced payments professionals and build high-integrity relationships with experts in the fieldMust have a strong network across the payments industry across different markets in MENAMust have proven talent ‘closing’ relationships and growing a businessMust have proven innovation experience including bringing new-to-industry products to marketMust be both a curious student and a patient teacherMust have experience in a fast growing small organizationMust have great communication skillsLanguage fluency in an Arabic is requiredMust be based in Dubai, UAE


Package And Benefits

Great compensation package, including performance‑based commissionMedical insurance for you and your familyLife insuranceMobile allowanceEmployee wellbeing & support programLearning & Development budgetWork‑life balance (flexibility and hybrid working policy)Our team is global: you will work with 65+ different nationalities spread across 5 continents


Sounds like you?

data.title: Senior Manager Operational Demand Planning EM
data.jd_text:
Purpose And Relevance For The Organization

Your task as Senior Manager Operational Demand Planning (ODP) EM, is to establish the Operational Demand Planning process across EM. As the ODP Rollout across EM continues, there is a need to setup and train teams, setup systems (APO) and establish processes within clusters to support active demand planning.

Our first objective is to lay down a first Rolling Demand Plan (RDP) by recruiting ODP Teams, setting up APO (legacy) and building a robust MILO/DILO/SILO process to support the demand signalling for supply planning and the purchasing of inventory. The second part of this process is to support the actualization of the plan by measuring the buying volumes (MFC vs MBV) to forecast, followed by the utilization of Inventory in season (to improve profitability and cashflow)

Once we have established a basic level of Operational Demand Planning, the focus shifts to centralized decision support by building better analytics and create the link between global DP/SP and the clusters within EM.

Future Planning Solution (FPS) will be the next step as we move into our new environment using the established Operational Demand Planning skills and processes to make the shift into the end to end solution more seamless.

Responsibilities

Work with existing ODP Clusters to further bed-down the Planning processes Establish teams in outstanding ODP Clusters – PAC, Turkey, IsrealWorking with global and local stakeholders, actively participate in the assessment and gap analysis, to understand the future set up in clusters and HUBSetup APO system with relevant logic and ensure system is soundLeverage expertise to determine the ODP processManage the change across Clusters to ensure relevant stakeholders are engagedProvide regular updates to senior stakeholders on ODP RolloutWith global and regional input and support, drive change management by ensuring all stakeholders are aware project status and future changes. Determine and propose future demand planning org. needs, considering existing processes and resources, and strategic role and set up of demand planning FPS. With the head of EM DP, implement the Operational Demand Planning strategy across clusters.Build the resources and expertise and E2E processes in clusters to drive article planning, better carry over management and inventory control, closely linked to IBP.With ODP in place, support key projects like Read and React, NOS, PR and nearshoring.

Other

Process ownership:Align on E2E and R&R by clusterProcess training of teams and functions Readiness for Key user training and testing Future S&OP process with stakeholders 
KPIs

Global Demand Planning KPIsInventory managementAvailabilityAPO rollout in EM

Key Contacts

CTC DTC merchandizing SCMSales Finance / IBPGlobal Demand Planning / ITCluster Demand Planning

Knowledge, Skills And Abilities

Strong project management skillsComfort around senior managementChallenging the status quoStrategic thinkerChange Management Demand Planning experience Strong analytical and numerical skillsSAP knowledge (APO, AFS)Advanced MS Excel skillsGood knowledge of MS Office (PP, Word) Excellent communication skills Fluent in EnglishAdidas experience a benefit

Educational Background/Minimum Requirements

Bachelor’s degreeMinimum 8-10 years experience in demand planning3-5 years project management experience

adidas celebrates diversity, supports inclusiveness and encourages individual expression in our workplace. We do not tolerate the harassment or discrimination toward any of our applicants or employees. We are an equal opportunity employer.

Talan is a global consulting group specializing in innovation and transformation through technology. For over 20 years, we have partnered with companies and public organizations to drive meaningful change.

We are purpose-driven, working across diverse sectors to tackle social and environmental challenges, simplify and improve market operations, leverage digital transformation, and ensure that data is managed responsibly and ethically.

Our expertise spans consultancy and outsourcing services, including programme management, market design, and governance. We also offer award-winning capabilities in cyber security, data privacy, and digital transformation.

The CTO will define the technical strategy and architecture for the Smart Metering Programme, ensuring all systems—Meters, communications services, HES, MDMS, and IT components—are secure, scalable, and interoperable. data.title: Field CTO
data.jd_text:
Introduction

At IBM Global Sales, we bring together innovation, collaboration, and expertise to help clients solve their most complex business challenges. Working across industries and geographies, you’ll partner with colleagues, clients, and partners to co-create solutions that drive digital transformation and lasting impact.Success in Global Sales is built on curiosity, empathy, and collaboration. You’ll connect technical understanding with strong people skills, building trusted relationships and shaping solutions that improve business and society. With world-class onboarding, continuous learning, and a supportive culture, IBM offers the tools and opportunities to grow your career. Join us and be part of a global team that’s passionate about driving innovation and making a difference.

Your Role And Responsibilities

The Field CTO role is a high-impact position passionate about executive-level engagement, field enablement, and strategic product feedback. As a Field CTO, you act as an extension of the CTO and CEO to strategically enable and support sales teams by building pipelines, closing opportunities, and maintaining long-term relationships with customer leadership teams. You are a trusted advisor to our customers, providing business, technical, and organizational guidance and thought leadership to drive customer outcomes. You work closely with regional sales leadership to focus on top-priority accounts within the theater with significant pipeline impact. As the technical leader and executive sponsor for key accounts, you deeply understand the customer's technical landscape and can articulate how HashiCorp, an IBM company's (HashiCorp) products drive their business success. You also broadly influence the strategic direction for customer messaging through collaboration with the Strategy team, R&D, the broader field, and industry events. You are a trusted advisor to our customers, providing business, technical, and thought leadership to drive adoption and success with our products.

Illustrating the impact of the HashiCorp product suite in delivering customer outcomes is your strength, and you showcase your thought leadership and technical expertise through participation in webinars, speaking events, round tables, white papers, marketing events, advisory boards, and executive briefings. Dynamic communication and presentation skills amongst varied audiences of both business and technology leaders are essential for success in this role, and you should be as adept at engaging with the customer’s C-level executives as you are working closely with internal product and engineering teams.

As a Field CTO, you contribute to public and internal messaging of the HashiCorp principles and tech vision to existing and potential customer executives and decision-makers and enable internal teams and partners to elevate the product narrative. As a field CTO, you are regarded as a business and technical thought leader at HashiCorp and a well-rounded technologist in the IT/application operations landscape.

In This Role You Can Expect To

Work closely with field marketing teams to spread awareness and support pipeline building via customer touch points (round tables, workshops, etc), conferences, strategic events, blogs, webinars, and whitepapers, among other opportunities. Aggregate and synthesize customer feedback on product messaging, pricing, and capabilities and communicate those findings to the relevant internal organizations (sales, marketing, R&D, enablement, services).Coordinate with the regional sales team to establish relevant relationships and act as a technical lead and trusted advisor to accelerate the pipeline for strategic accounts.Understand the customer's technical, people, and process needs, and articulate how HashiCorp's products can meet those needs for the enterprise.Work closely with the field teams to identify opportunities for product adoption, expansion, and extension within their accounts, to communicate a unified vision, and to collaborate through thought leadership.Collaborate with product and marketing teams to provide feedback on customer needs and struggles to ensure our products' proper future direction and success.Drive customer CISO/CxO engagements with the field teams.Validate and contribute to technical materials, standard methodologies, etc., based on field experience to continuously up-level and enable our internal partners.Lead executive briefings and advisory boards with customers, partners, and prospects.Educate, mentor, and promote upskilling within the sales and customer success teams on HashiCorp's products through internal enablement efforts.

Preferred Education

Bachelor's Degree

Required Technical And Professional Expertise

You must have an Engineering/CS/MIS Bachelor’s Degree or strong relevant industry experience;Have a history of executive engagement or served at an executive level;Enjoy speaking in front of crowds, from small to very large, virtually;Are a well-rounded

data.title: Vice President of Global Expansion

UnlockLand is an AI-driven PropTech company recognized as one of the top innovators in the “Future of Lifestyle” PropTech Accelerator, led by the National Information Technology Development Program (NITDP) and the Real Estate General Authority (REGA) under Saudi Vision 2030.
 Our mission is to transform how developers, investors, and governments plan, evaluate, and optimize real-estate assets. 
UnlockLand’s AI Operating System integrates geospatial data, financial modelling, and regulatory intelligence to deliver instant feasibility studies and predictive analytics — helping cities and investors build smarter, more sustainable communities.
As we scale across North America, the Middle East, and Asia, UnlockLand is seeking a Vice President of Finance to lead our financial strategy, operational excellence, and investment planning. This executive leader will ensure the company’s financial structure supports rapid global expansion, enterprise partnerships, and large-scale real estate intelligence programs.
As we expand across North America, the Middle East, and Asia, UnlockLand is seeking a Vice President of Operations & Global Expansion to architect scalable systems, optimize cross-functional execution, and drive operational excellence across continents. This leader will ensure UnlockLand’s operational backbone supports rapid adoption, enterprise partnerships, and national-level PropTech initiatives.
This is a high-impact role for an operations executive with deep experience in fast-growth SaaS/AI environments, international expansion, and real estate or smart-city ecosystems.

data.title: Transition Portfolio Manager, Vice President
data.jd_text:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking candidates with 10+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics.

This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office.

Why this role is important to us

State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter

This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As a Transition Portfolio Manager, you will:

Utilize your in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle (pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects) to lead portfolio restructures for KSA-domiciled clients including complex multi-manager, multi-asset class events.Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions).Cover trade reconciliation process between internal systems for accuracy and completeness as needed prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades.Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert on multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies.Apply your expertise on benchmarking and measurement of transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA)Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topicsAssess internal business operations (trading strategies, performance, technology, data management, marketing, research) and develop solutions to enhance product positioningLead and contribute to strategic projects in partnership with other groups / businesses within State Street as well as external vendorsAssist in evolving and industrializing our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors


What We Value

The following skills will help you succeed in this role:

Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classesFamiliarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / toolsClient-facing experience leading complex transitions or portfolio implementations; strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsTechnical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposesSelf-starter with ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on detailsStrong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team


Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar10+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational roleMBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or CFA designation preferred


Additional Requirements

CMA registration requiredWillingness to travel up to 10% of the timeExperience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus


About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to

data.title: IT Operations Section Head
data.jd_text:
The IT Operations Section Head supervises the provision of IT services at SANS, ensuring they meet user requirements and business goals. Duties include overseeing service desk operations, formulating SLAs, managing incidents, problem, and change management processes, and liaising with internal teams and external vendors to uphold service standards, address issues, and drive enhancements.
Key Qualifications and Requirements – Minimum of +7 years of experience in IT service management.A bachelor’s degree in software engineering, Engineering, IT, Computer Science, Management Information Systems (MIS), or a related business/technical field is required. ITIL certified. PMP certification is a plus.
Key Technical SkillsDemonstrates technical expertise and hands-on proficiency across various IT operations domains, encompassing networking, system administration, storage management, cloud computing, service management, cybersecurity, and infrastructure automation.Proficient in utilizing service management tools like ServiceNow, BMC, or Jira, with prior experience in implementation, configuration, and optimization.Skilled in harnessing automation features within service management tools to streamline processes, automate routine tasks, and enhance operational efficiency.Competent in utilizing service management tools to establish workflows and integrate with other systems via APIs.Adept at utilizing service management tools to facilitate incident and problem management, change and release management, knowledge management, and service catalog management.Understanding of Configuration Management Database (CMDB) and asset management principles, processes, and tools to effectively track and manage IT assets and configurations.Proficient in utilizing reporting and analytics functions within service management tools to monitor service performance, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and identify trends or areas for improvement.Capable of designing Service Level Agreements (SLAs) that are clear, measurable, and attainable, considering service levels, performance metrics, responsibilities, and escalation procedures.Able to monitor service performance against SLA targets using KPIs and metrics.Skilled in integrating SLAs into vendor contracts and effectively managing relationships with external service providers.Proficient in creating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and system documentation efficiently.

data.title: Head, KAUST Innovation Ventures
data.company: KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology)
data.jd_text:
Position SummaryKAUST Innovation Ventures (KIV) is an impact investment fund that provides seed and early-stage funding to technology-based startups founded by KAUST faculty, researchers, students and staff. KIV also invests in high-profile spin-ins, and in other early-stage technology startups in Saudi Arabia.
The Head of the fund will play a critical role to drive the fund’s strategy, execute high potential investments derived from the university’s innovation pipeline, actively raise co-investment capital from strategic partners, as well as advise on and architect exit transactions for mature assets.
The role requires a blend of deep investment acumen, experience supporting deep technology companies and research spinouts, entrepreneurial insights, connectivity to the VC ecosystem, and a commitment to institutional grade governance.
Major ResponsibilitiesContribute to the Fund Strategy: Help define and refine the fund's investment thesis, sector focus, and deployment strategy and follow-on funding, ensuring alignment with the university's mission and commercial objectives.Ecosystem Leadership: Act as a key liaison between the fund, the entrepreneurship center, university faculty, research labs, tech transfer office, and the external VC/corporate community.Pipeline Generation: Develop a robust, proprietary deal flow from university-affiliated startups, spin-offs, high potential spin-ins, impact ventures and early-stage companies.
Investment Execution & Due Diligence Best PracticesParticipate in the investment process for strategic deals but more importantly, ensure that the investment team (investment managers and associates) employ best practices across the investment cycle including:Initial screening: market analysis, financial modeling, valuation, and structuring of deal terms.Technical Due Diligence: looking at intellectual property ownership, technology advantage, competitive landscape, and inherent technology risk across future milestones.Commercial & Financial Due Diligence: thorough commercial, financial, and legal due diligence on prospective investments, presenting clear and concise investment recommendations to the Investment Committee.Negotiation & Closing: Lead the negotiation of complex transaction documents (e.g., term sheets, shareholder agreements) to secure favorable terms for the fund and co-investors.
Portfolio Value Creation& OversightDeploy Best Practices: Develop, deploy, and maintain value creation best practices across the portfolio. This includes implementing operational improvements, optimizing go-to-market strategies, leveraging university partnerships and synergies as well and enhancing financial planning/reporting for portfolio companies.Strategic Support: Act as a hands-on resource for portfolio company management teams, identifying key talent needs, supporting critical commercial contracts, and navigating strategic challenges.Risk Mitigation: Proactively monitor portfolio company performance against key milestones and financial targets, initiating corrective actions where necessary.Team and Board composition: Proactively find solutions to support the portfolio companies to augment and enhance their executive team and capabilities as well as enhance their governance and strategic direction.
Governance, Controls, & ReportingCompliance & Controls: Establish and enforce best-in-class governance and control standards for all fund activities, ensuring strict adherence to legal, regulatory, and fiduciary obligations.Reporting Excellence: Oversee and prepare accurate, timely, and professional reporting(e.g., quarterly investor reports, performance metrics, valuation summaries) for the Investment Committee and co-investment partners.Technology Adoption: Promote and support the use of technology tools such as CRM’s, Newsletters, Portfolio Dashboards …etc. to track, report and manage the progress of portfolio companies across the investment team.
Capital Attraction & Custodianship for Co-InvestmentPosition KAUST as a trusted custodian of capital and as a first choice for strategic partners including ministries, large corporates and semi-governmental entities to commit funding to be managed and deployed into the KSA ecosystem by KAUST Investment Ventures.Strategic Partner Engagement: Proactively identify, build, and maintain strong relationships with potential strategic co-investment partners (e.g., corporations, institutional investors).Syndication: Structure and execute co-investment opportunities, ensuring seamless communication and alignment with external capital partners.Multi-Vehicle reporting: Enable reporting to key stakeholders and co-investors in a seamless way across multiple investment vehicles and multiple pools of capital.
Exit Strategy & DivestmentPortfolio Prioritization: Maintaining and up to date status on ventures in the portfolio to understand which companies are ideal for favorable exist and emergency exits as well.Exit Architecture: Actively manage and facilitate or

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text:
مطلوب: رئيس مالي (CFO) يتحدث العربية — لشركة تطوير عقاري مختصة بالضيافة | الرياض عميلنا، شركة رائدة في تطوير العقار الفندقي داخل المملكة، يبحث عن رئيس مالي ذو خبرة عالية للانضمام إلى فريق القيادة التنفيذية والعمل بشكل مباشر مع مجلس الإدارة على تطوير الحوكمة المالية، وإدارة التدفقات النقدية، وتمويل الأصول والمشاريع.

المهام والمسؤوليات الأساسية القيادة المالية الاستراتيجية
العمل مع مجلس الإدارة لتعزيز الحوكمة وتطوير الاستراتيجية المالية طويلة المدى.الإشراف على هيكلة رأس المال، القرارات الاستثمارية، وإدارة المخاطر المالية عبر محفظة مشاريع الضيافة.إدارة التدفقات النقدية والأداء
قيادة مراجعات التدفقات النقدية التشغيلية بشكل شهري مع فريق العمل.رفع الملاحظات مبكرًا واقتراح حلول عملية لضمان السيولة واستقرار الأداء المالي.التمويل وجمع رأس المال
التواصل مع البنوك، المؤسسات المالية، والصناديق الاستثمارية لتمويل المشاريع القادمة.بناء وإدارة علاقات قوية مع الجهات التمويلية والمستثمرين.الرقابة المالية والتقارير
تعزيز نظم الرقابة المالية وضمان الالتزام بالسياسات والمعايير الداخلية والخارجية.ضمان إصدار تقارير مالية دقيقة وفي الوقت المحدد من خلال وضع جداول واضحة وتحديد مسؤوليات الفريق.المتطلباتخبرة قيادية مالية داخل السوق السعودي.خلفية قوية في العقار، الضيافة، التمويل الاستثماري، أو مجالات مشابهة.خبرة مثبتة في جمع التمويل، إدارة التدفقات النقدية، والحوكمة المؤسسية.قدرة على العمل مع مجالس الإدارة وقيادات عليا في بيئة نمو متسارع.للتقديم إذا كنت قائدًا ماليًا طموحًا وتبحث عن دور مؤثر في شركة تطوير ضيافة نامية داخل المملكة، يسعدنا التواصل معك.
قدّم الآن أو تواصل معنا مباشرة بسرية تامة.

data.title: Traditional Trade Channel Head
data.jd_text:
About the Role:

Ferrero is seeking a dynamic Traditional Trade Channel Head to lead our strategy and growth in the traditional trade sector; covering independent retailers, wholesalers, and neighborhood shops. In this role, you will be responsible for building strong customer relationships, driving perfect store execution, expanding numeric distribution, and delivering commercial excellence across this critical channel.

Main Responsibilities:

As a Traditional Trade Channel Manager, you will develop and execute the Traditional Trade strategy to drive market share and penetration. You will manage relationships with key partners, distributors, and wholesalers to ensure alignment and deliver high service levels throughout the channel. Your responsibilities will include leading field execution plans in general trade outlets to secure product visibility, availability, and pricing compliance. You will also oversee promotional planning and trade investments, optimizing return on investment and maximizing in-market impact.

Collaboration with marketing, category management, and supply chain teams is crucial to support product launches and guarantee consistent activation in the market. Additionally, you will analyze channel performance, customer data, and market trends to recommend and drive growth initiatives. Key performance indicators for this position include volume and revenue growth in traditional trade, numeric distribution and customer expansion, execution compliance for visibility, availability, and pricing, distributor performance and stock availability, as well as the return on investment for trade investments and promotions.

You will lead your team by setting objectives, managing performance, and facilitating training. You are responsible for compliance, efficient resource management, conflict resolution, and fostering a positive, collaborative culture to support productivity and employee growth.

About You:

The ideal candidate will have a bachelor’s degree in business, Sales, or Marketing and at least 10 to 12 years of experience in FMCG sales management, with a particular focus on traditional or general trade.

You have a strong understanding of distributor networks, local market dynamics, and channel execution is required. Experience in sweet snacks, confectionery, or impulse categories is an advantage.

Proficiency in sales analytics, Excel, PowerPoint, and trade spend tools is important for success in this role.

We seek someone with strong interpersonal and negotiation skills, who is commercially driven with a consumer-first mindset. To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate excellent planning, execution, and follow-up abilities. Resilience, adaptability, and proactive work habits are key.

You excel at managing and developing people. Your ability to nurture talent and inspire confidence helps create an environment where individuals thrive and teams achieve remarkable goals.

Our Benefits & Perks:

Careers with caring built in - discover our benefits here.

About Ferrero:

Ferrero began its journey in the small town of Alba in Piedmont, Italy, in 1946. Today, it is one of the world’s largest sweet-packaged food companies, with many iconic brands sold in countries all over the world. Find out more about Ferrero at ferrero.com.

DE&I at Ferrero:

Ferrero is committed to building a diverse and inclusive culture in which all employees feel welcomed and appreciated and have the same opportunities. We believe all our people are equally talented in their own way. In nurturing the curiosity and natural abilities of our employees, we provide them, generation after generation, the means to succeed personally and professionally, enabling them to craft their journey at Ferrero. The diversity of our talents is what makes our work environment multicultural, innovative, and highly rewarding.

Find out more here.

data.title: QNB3601 - Vice President Domestic Compliance Strategy & Supervision
data.jd_text:
About QNB

Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.

QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries across three continents providing a comprehensive range of advanced products and services. The total number of employees is more than 28,000 serving up to 20 million customers operating through 1,000 locations, with an ATM network of 4,300 machines.

QNB has maintained its position as one of the highest rated regional banks from leading credit rating agencies including Standard & Poor’s (A), Moody’s (Aa3) and Fitch (A+). The Bank has also been the recipient of many awards from leading international specialised financial publications.

Based on the Group’s consistent strong financial performance and its expanding international presence, QNB currently ranks as the most valuable bank brand in the Middle East and Africa, according to Brand Finance Magazine.

QNB Group has an active community support program and sponsors various social, educational and sporting events.

Job Purpose Summary

The incumbent will be responsible for conducting/managing the compliance function for the Group’s domestic Strategic and supervisory Activities on a pro-active basis covering both Business and Regulatory domestic activities of all Business divisions & Subsidiaries operating in the State of Qatar. All efforts should be made to identify and assess the compliance risks associated with domestic activities of Business, Support and Operations divisions & Local Subsidiaries in light of applicable regulations of QCB, QFMA, QFCRA, QE and any other applicable statutory requirements, Group policies/procedures and industry standards and best practices.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities By Dimensions

 Shareholder & Financial: Identify and assess the Regulatory Compliance risk involved in the activities of Business, Support and Operations divisions & Local Subsidiaries conducted by QNB Group in the state of Qatar and provide the strategic and supervisory vision, directions and solutions to both Domestic and operations regulatory compliance departments and thereby reduce the risk of regulatory sanctions, which could result in financial losses/ penal actions and the associated damage of reputation of QNB Group Implements KPI’s and best practices for VP, Domestic Compliance Strategy and Supervision Promote cost consciousness and efficiency and enhance productivity, to minimise cost, avoid waste, and optimise benefits for the bank. Act within the limits of the powers delegated to the incumbent and delegate authority to the respective staff and monitor exercise of the same. Demonstrate clear understanding of the important factors behind the bank's financial & non-financial performance Customer (Internal & External): Assist the EVP Domestic Compliance in Setting-up the compliance strategy for the domestic activities including Business, Support and Operations. Assist the EVP Domestic Compliance in identifying the strategic issues and the related solutions resulting from the performance of the tasks/activities as per the Group’s annual compliance plan for the domestic activities of Business, Support and Operations divisions & Local Subsidiaries. Provide timely feedback and support to the EVP Domestic Compliance and to the Group Chief Compliance Officer on ad hoc matters, short or mid-terms projects to achieve the Group Compliance objectives as and when directed. Manage, guide and instruct all direct report staff to ensure meeting individual and departmental objectives and monitor their compliance with internal policies, rules and code of ethics and conduct. Maintain close contact across internal departments in order to anticipate and proactively address compliance issues as well as disseminate compliance culture. Provide all the necessary support to all business, Support & Operations divisions and Local subsidiaries to achieve their business objectives related to their domestic activities in line with the regulatory requirements as and when required. Provide information/inputs with regards to the Strategic plans on the domestic activities of Group’s Business and operations Divisions & Local Subsidiaries to the EVP Domestic Compliance and to the Group Chief Compliance Officer in the former’s absence to resolve the related strategic queries/issues raised by the regulatory authorities including but not limited to the following:Qatar Central Bank.Qatar Financial Market Authority Qatar Exchange. Qatar Financial Center Regulatory Authority.Other government bodies in Qatar like Ministries and General Authorities. On quarterly basis, collect/provide input data and information of the strategic activities performed related to all Business, Support and operations divisions & Local Subsidiaries to the EVP Domestic Compliance in order to construct Management reports by the

data.title: Head, IT Support
data.company: University of Doha for Science & Technology
data.jd_text:
Overview

University of Doha for Science and Technology (UDST) was officially established by the Emiri Decision No13 of 2022, and it is the first national university specializing in academic applied, technical, and professional education in the State of Qatar. UDST has over 70 bachelor's and master's degree programs, diplomas, and certificates. The university houses 5 colleges: The College of Business, the College of Computing and Information Technology, the College of Engineering and Technology, the College of Health Sciences, and the College of General Education, in addition to specialized training centers for individuals and companies. UDST is recognized for its student-centered learning and state-of-the-art facilities. Its world-renowned faculty and researchers work on developing the students’ skills and help raise well-equipped graduates who proudly serve different sectors of the economy and contribute to achieving human, social, and economic development goals nationally and internationally.

With more than 700 staff and over 8,000 students, UDST is the destination of choice for applied and experiential learning. The University is recognized for its student-centered learning and state-of-the-art facilities. Our faculty are committed to delivering pedagogically-sound learning experiences that incorporate innovative learning technologies. Our aim is to enhance students’ skills and help develop talented graduates who can effectively contribute to a knowledge-based economy and make Qatar’s National Vision 2030 a reality.

Responsibilities

The successful candidate will support the development of the Information Division’s business plan in alignment with UDST strategy and future technology needs through its projects and initiatives.

Operational:

Service Desk Management: Oversee the University's IT service desk operations, ensuring timely and effective response to user inquiries and technical issues while monitoring service levels, response times, and customer satisfaction.

Incident Resolution: Streamline operational procedures and employ proactive problem-solving strategies to accelerate the timely and accurate resolution of IT incidents and service requests, minimizing user disruption while identifying trends and root causes to implement preventive measures.

User Training and Self-Help Resources: Develop and deliver training sessions and resources to empower users to resolve common technical issues independently and maintain a knowledge base of solutions and troubleshooting guides.

Vendor and Contract Management: Strategically partner with vendors and service providers to resolve escalated technical issues and manage service agreements and procure hardware, software and services required for IT support.

Continuous Improvement: Challenge the status quo of support processes, identify areas for improvement and implement strategies to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of support services.

User Communication: Establish relationship with University stakeholders to engage and communicate effectively on updates on service disruptions, maintenance, technical issues and manage user expectations and maintain a high level of customer service.

Budgeting and Resource Allocation: Manage the IT Support sections’ budget, allocating resources for staffing, training, tools, and equipment to optimize resource utilization to meet user needs and service levels.

Documentation and Reporting: Safeguard critical records of incidents, service requests and resolutions to enable decision-making by tracking performance, trends and user feedback.

Perform miscellaneous job-related duties as assigned.

Provide professional development support and perform periodic performance reviews for direct subordinates based on UDST’s Employee Performance Management framework to ensure continual high performance of employees in alignment with UDST's mission and mandate.

Qualifications

 Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field required.  Master’s degree/ professional certification is preferred.  Minimum 7 years of relevant experience in IT in a Higher Education Institutes.  Fluency in written and spoken English language is required.  Fluency in written and spoken Arabic language is preferred.

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text:
Job SummaryThe Chief Financial Officer (CFO) provides strategic financial leadership to ensure the organization’s financial stability, profitability, and long-term growth. The role oversees financial planning, reporting, capital management, risk governance, and financial strategy while supporting executive decision-making and driving operational efficiency across the organization.
